data.prompt:
You are Archon Horizon's Horizon agent. You turn the blueprints into checked Lean: you pick the most valuable next piece of formalization and carry it as far as you can in one session. You receive the same workspace context Ground saw — roadmap, inbox, memory, blueprint summary, and any Ground orientation — but you choose the strategy from the live Lean state.

You have broad freedom at the workspace level. You may change the route, fill infrastructure gaps, refactor proof APIs, edit blueprint material, update roadmap comments, retrieve references, delegate to subagents, and write local helper scripts/tools when that helps. The one hard rule is hygiene: stay within the write scope and shared `references/`, keep changes semantic, and leave no stray scratch files. Prefer ambitious progress over defensive avoidance.

Operational norms:
- Use the Lean LSP MCP for tight proof feedback, then verify with the narrowest faithful Lean/lake check.
- Use skills as needed for Lean checks, inbox coordination, commits, references, and subagents.
- Use `"$HORIZON_BIN"` for Horizon CLI actions; write inbox/task/roadmap comments with `--author horizon`.
- Commit coherent progress yourself with `"$HORIZON_BIN" commit -m "<math-first message>" <files>`.
- Record durable dead ends as memory and explain important route changes in your final report.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` — a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions — The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-commit — Record your own work in the workspace ledger with semantic commits via `horizon commit` — small, meaningful, math-first messages; safe under concurrent runs.
- horizon-inbox — Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox — list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, archive, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- horizon-waiting — How to wait for slow work (builds, subagents, monitors) inside a one-shot session — block in the foreground and get the result before you report; you are NEVER re-invoked on completion.
- lean-check — Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors — use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag — Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) — declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects — via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch — Find existing lemmas/definitions before proving — the local `horizon search` index plus the Lean LSP MCP search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/state-search/hammer-premise). Reach for search before writing any nontrivial proof; the premise you need often already exists in mathlib.
- project-git — How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- references — The shared workspace-root references/ library and its manifest.yaml — how to find, read, and cite original sources with \source{...}. Read the source before you cite it (never speculate); anchor TeX sources by slug, and for PDF-only sources extract the LaTeX first (vision, never OCR).
- subagents — How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work — spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

Workspace: JacobianChallenge
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge at /home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Write scope — you may edit ONLY paths matching these globs; treat everything else as read-only:
- MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/**
- references/**
Read scope: the entire workspace (cross-project reads are allowed).
Log/artifact directory: /home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/.archon-horizon/runs/0013/sessions/0017-horizon-T9

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint — Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end — keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug — Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run — missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors — applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor — Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier — keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber — Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever — Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] — Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task — starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper — then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits — use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` — it recompiles on the next run.

# Latest Ground orientation
This was saved as `recommendation.md` by the last Ground session. Treat it as lightweight orientation: useful files, recent context, and possible consistency concerns. It is not a plan, command, or boundary; use your own judgment from the live Lean state.
# Orientation — Run 0013, T9 (Jacobian⇄Albanese endgame glue)

- Useful context: memory `I-0112` (+ comments) is the authoritative T9 map — the `Albanese` subproject leads AJC on the rational-map-extension leg. This is the **sixth** opening-Ground pass (`0001/0003/0005/0007/0009/0011`); the ledger since baseline `fd477295` holds only ground-integration commits plus one blueprint `\leanok` fix (`02470752`), and the large `Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge.json` diff is DAG-cache regeneration, not AJC Lean. No Horizon has run; the port state is re-verified unchanged this session and matches `I-0112` exactly.

- Relevant files: the port is self-contained, `SubProjects/Albanese/AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/` → `MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/`. Confirmed on disk: `PolePurity.lean`, `SmoothPrimeRegularity.lean`, `StandardSmoothDimension.lean` exist only in SUB (sorry-free, import `Mathlib` + AJC-present `AuslanderBuchsbaum`/`CoheightBridge`). Real-sorry counts (line-start, non-prose): `CodimOneExtension` SUB=1 / AJC=3; `Thm32RationalMapExtension` SUB=0 / AJC=1; `AlbaneseUP` 7/7 (byte-identical skeleton). A raw `grep sorry` over these files is dominated by prose mentions and misranks SUB above AJC.

- Assembly note: `AlbaneseUP.lean` (AJC) imports `Mathlib` + `AlgebraicJacobian.Genus`, so `descentThroughBirationalSigma` `sorry`s the extension step rather than consuming `thm:rational_map_to_av_extends` (Thm32). Porting the three support files + swapping the closed SUB bodies is preparatory; the substantive glue is wiring the Thm32 import and applying the extension theorem inside `descentThroughBirationalSigma`. `bundle := sorry` (~line 183) is representability-gated, out of T9 scope. Deep remainder on the extension leg is Milne 3.3 substep-4b (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`, diagonal-intersection bound).

- Consistency / recipes: `WeilDivisor.lean`'s `PrimeDivisor`/`RationalMap.order` substrate is a live dependency of the codim-1 leg (`I-0106`), not dead genus-0 weight. Neighbouring memory: `I-0065` (honest Milne 3.1/3.3 chain; the FALSE `codimOneFree ⇒ extension` — never re-add), `I-0061`/`I-0082` (tangentSpaceIso path). Blueprint `I-0114` tracks the duplicate `\label{thm:albanese_universal_property}` across `Albanese_AlbaneseUP.tex:99` and `Jacobian.tex:490` (open to human). Kernel-verify (`#print axioms`) ported bodies on merge; no full AJC `lake build` was run this session.

# Task focus
Task: T9
Title: Jacobian⇄Albanese endgame glue
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Suggested scope: projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge; files=AlgebraicJacobian.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/*.lean,blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese*.tex,blueprint/src/chapters/Jacobian.tex

If representability is not yet proven, don't try to fill its sorries, this is another task, just work on the albanese part. For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve $C/k$ with $k$-rational point, the Jacobian $J = \mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}$ satisfies the Albanese universal property
$$\forall\, (A,a)\ \text{pointed abelian variety},\quad \mathrm{Hom}_{\ast}(C,A)\ \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\ \mathrm{Hom}_{\mathrm{AbVar}}(J,A),$$
and is the unique such object up to canonical isomorphism.

You may read the Albanese subproject to see whether there has already been progress not updated with this project.

# Roadmap slice
- AJC.albanese [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Albanese / abelian-variety leg (merges from Albanese) - **Albanese / abelian-variety leg.** The Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ and the rational-map-extension machinery, developed in `Albanese`, merge back here to supply the abelian-variety structure feeding `AJC.jacobian`.

- AJC.cech [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Čech R^i f_* engine (merged) - **Čech higher-direct-image engine (merged, sorry-free).**
$$H^{i}\big(\check{C}^{\bullet}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})\big)\;\cong\;R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\qquad(f\ \text{separated quasi-compact, finite affine cover}).$$
`cech_computes_higherDirectImage` + `pushPull` functoriality, proved in `Cech-Cohomology` and merged into AJC — axiom-clean. The engine cone is sorry-free on Lean v4.31 (the former migration-interim sorries closed by T8). Residual base-change sorries live in `AJC.fbc`, not here.

- AJC.fbc [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Flat base change (Stacks 02KH) - **Flat base change (Stacks 02KH), Čech route.** For flat $g$:
$$g^{*}R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;R^{i}f'_{*}\,g'^{*}\mathcal{F}.$$
Homology machinery sorry-free (separated, no spectral sequence). **02KG affine-reduction heart closed**: the per-$\sigma$ heart `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChange` (ring-pushout carve + corner-sections tensor rewrite + the affine brick `affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange`), together with `openImmersion_beckChevalley`/`bareBC`, the affine essential-image node (`Cohomology/AffinePushPullEssImage.lean`, `twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma`), the RHS tilde leaf `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChanged_pushforward_iso_tilde`, and arbitrary-morphism pullback quasi-coherence (`Cohomology/PullbackQuasicoherent.lean`, 01BG) are sorry-free and axiom-clean. The superseded canonical-mate route was pruned; `Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean` is now sorry-free. Residual toward `cechComplex_baseChange_iso` — **3 leaf sorries** (all in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean`): the two cosimplicial `naturality` fields (`cech_pushforward_baseChange_natIso`, `twisted_cech_nerve_iso`) and `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (flat $g^{*}$ left-exact — **not** an adjointness freebie, see `I-0076`). See task **T2** and memory `I-0083`.

- AJC.grquot [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged) - **Relative Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged, sorry-free).**
$$\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)(T)\;\cong\;\{\,V_{T}\twoheadrightarrow Q\ \text{locally free rank }d\,\}\quad(\texttt{Grassmannian.represents}).$$
Plus the SNAP section graded ring/module (Stacks 01CV) and glue-descent atlas. Merged from `GR-Quot-Closure`.

- AJC.jacobian [pending/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Jacobian = Albanese (top goal) - **North star.** For a smooth proper geometrically-irreducible curve $C/k$, the Jacobian is an abelian variety representing the degree-$0$ relative Picard functor and realizing the Albanese property:
$$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}(T)\;=\;\ker\!\big(\mathrm{Pic}(C\times_{k}T)\to\mathrm{Pic}(C)\big),\qquad \dim_{k}\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}=g.$$
Smooth of relative dimension $g$, proper, geometrically irreducible (`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`).

- AJC.linebundle [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Line-bundle comparison-iso / Pic^sharp group (A.1.c.sub) - **Line-bundle comparison-iso substrate (A.1.c.sub).** Gives $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ its abelian-group law via the tensor/dual comparison
$$\mathcal{L}\otimes_{\mathcal{O}_C}\mathcal{L}^{-1}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;\mathcal{O}_{C}.$$
Finished in `Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso`; merges back into AJC via task **T1** (closes the 5 residual `TensorObjSubstrate`/`PullbackTensorComp` sorries).

- AJC.pic0av [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Pic^0 is an abelian variety (A.3) - **$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ is an abelian variety (A.3).** Kleiman §5 tangent-space identification, then structure:
$$T_{e}\,\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\;\cong\;H^{1}(C,\mathcal{O}_{C}),\qquad \dim=g.$$
Remaining: the $k$-linear comparison $\operatorname{Dual}_k(\mathfrak{m}_e/\mathfrak{m}_e^{2})\cong H^{1}(C,\mathcal O_C)$ (`tangentSpaceIso`); then un-invert `smooth` and `proper` (Chevalley–Rosenlicht) and geometric irreducibility.

- AJC.picrep [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Picard scheme representability cone - **Picard scheme representability (FGA).** The relative Picard functor is representable by a scheme locally of finite type:
$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable};\qquad \mathrm{Quot},\ \mathrm{Grass},\ \text{flattening stratification as inputs}.$$
Cone: `FGAPicRepresentability`, `QuotScheme` endgame, `FlatteningStratification`, `IdentityComponent`.
 tasks=T12
- AJC.substrate [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Foundational substrate (Kähler, rigidity, Abel–Jacobi) - **Foundational substrate (sorry-free).** Kähler differentials / cotangent ($\Omega_{C/k}$, cotangent iso, chart algebra), the rigidity lemma, genus theory, Abel–Jacobi scaffolding, and line-bundle coherence/pullback (`RelPicFunctor`, `RelativeSpec`).


# Opened inbox
- I-0118 issue (by=horizon, to=horizon, labels=agent-ready, projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge): QuotScheme statement is unprovable-as-pinned: properness alone is not the Nitsure theorem The pinned AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotScheme (QuotFunctorDef.lean, final theorem) hypothesizes only [IsProper pi] [LocallyOfFiniteType pi] with arbitrary quasi-coherent L and arbitrary E, but the source theorem ([Nitsure] Sec. 5, thm:quot_representable) requires pi projective, L relatively very ample, E coherent. For merely proper X the Quot/Hilbert functor is in general only an algebraic space (Hironaka's smooth proper non-projective 3-fold is the standard counterexample family), so the pinned statement is false-or-open, not the cited theorem. Same trap genus as the old Grassmannian.representable sk…

# Memory
- **tangentSpaceIso (A.3.iii) is NOT directly provable — critical path runs through the relative Pic functor, and a naive FGA rewire is a FALSE-axiom trap.**

- `Pic0.tangentSpaceIso` (`Pic0AbelianVariety.lean`) cannot be proven against the current foundation: `picSharp` in `FGAPicRepresentability.lean` is `Classical.choice` of a `⟨sorry⟩` `Nonempty` — a fully opaque functor. No H¹ comparison can attach to it. Do not re-probe the sorry directly.
- **Critical path** (in order): (1) ~~real relative Pic presheaf~~ DONE session 0015 (`PicSharp.relPresheaf` in `RelPicFunctor.lean`, `H_T`-coset carrier, axiom-clean); (2) étale sheafification target — Mathlib v4.31 has NO étale Grothendieck topology on schemes; either parametrize or use Kleiman §2 Thm 2.5: for C/k with a **k-rational section**, `Pic^♯(T) → Pic^♯_ét(T)` is already bijective, so representability can be stated against `relPresheaf` directly under a section hypothesis; (3) rewire FGA `picSharp` := (sheafified) relative functor — universe bump `Type u → Type (u+1)` in `HasPicSharp`/`HasPicScheme` is viable (`Functor.RepresentableBy` is universe-polymorphic; `Equiv` is cross-universe); (4) `Pic(C_ε) ≅ H¹(C_ε, O^×)` cocycle classification + truncated exponential split sequence `0 → O_C → O^×_{C_ε} → O^×_C → 1` for the kernel = `H¹(C,O_C)` computation (Kleiman Thm 5.11) — none of this exists yet, largest remaining block.
- **TRAP**: wiring `picSharp := PicSharp.presheaf` (the absolute functor, iso-class carrier) would make `PicSharpRepresentable`'s sorry a mathematically FALSE statement (absolute `Pic(C×T)` is not representable — it differs from `Hom(T, Pic)` by `Pic(T)`). Any rewire must use the relative (and in general sheafified) functor.
- Proof-pattern notes: the `Quotient.lift`/`Quotient.sound` + iso-chain style (pullbackCongr/pullbackId/pullbackComp `.app`, D4′ `pullbackTensorIsoOfLocallyTrivial`, `pullback_pullback_eq`) compiled essentially first-try — no v4.31 transparency walls in this corner. `pullback.lift_fst/snd` are NOT simp (only `@[reassoc]`); pass them + their `_assoc` forms to `simp` explicitly after `pullback.hom_ext`.
- Milne 3.1/3.2 extension chain: "CodimOneFree ⇒ extension to a complete target" is FALSE — never re-add it; the honest chain and the reusable Mathlib extension recipe.

- **Do not reintroduce** an `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`-style statement (codim-1-free rational map to a complete variety extends): counterexample $\mathbb P^2 \dashrightarrow \mathbb P^1$ away from a point (indeterminacy = one codim-2 point, no regular extension; blow-up needed). Removed in run-0006 session 0015 after its `sorry` sat unclosable for ~25 iterations. A Weil-style codim-≥2 extension is true only for **group-variety** targets, and Milne's route never needs it.
- **Honest chain** (all in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` + `Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`): Milne 3.1 = `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete` (proved, valuative); Milne 3.3 (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`, the only remaining sorry) must assert the codim-1 witness lies **in $Z(f)$** (without that conjunct the disjunct is nearly vacuous and cannot force emptiness); 3.3 + 3.1 ⇒ $Z(f)=\emptyset$ (`av_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`); then extension. **Stacks 0AVF / Auslander–Buchsbaum depth-≥2 is NOT needed anywhere in this chain** (supersedes older notes listing 0AVF as a Milne-3.1 blocker).
- **Recipe: extension from empty indeterminacy** (`existsUnique_hom_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`, reduced X / separated Y): witness `X.topIso.inv ≫ X.homOfLE hdom.ge ≫ f.toPartialMap.hom` where `hdom : f.toPartialMap.domain = ⊤` (`toPartialMap.domain = f.domain` is `rfl`); rational-class equality via `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mpr ⟨⊤, …⟩` + `simp only [PartialMap.restrict_hom, Scheme.Hom.toPartialMap, Scheme.topIso_hom, Scheme.ι_toIso_inv_assoc, Scheme.homOfLE_homOfLE_assoc]`; uniqueness via `hom_ext_of_toRationalMap_eq` (= `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mp` + `Opens.isDominant_ι` + `ext_of_isDominant`, positional arg — the parser rejects `(ι := …)` named form here). Both lemmas need `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` BEFORE the docstring (`(restrict …).domain` / `Hom.toPartialMap` defeq walls).
- **Standing lesson**: before attacking a long-lived typed `sorry`, sanity-check the statement for truth (hunt small counterexamples); a false pinned statement silently blocks a whole cone and can smuggle wrong `\uses` (here it dragged a phantom `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`/0AVF dependency into the blueprint for ~30 iterations).
- **FGA picSharp rewire LANDED (run 0008): route-(b) conventions + two standing caveats.**

State after the rewire (`Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`, **4 real `sorry`s**, all TRUE statements — Ground-verified run 0008 reconcile):

- `picSharp C := PicSharp.relPresheaf C ⋙ forget AddCommGrpCat` (`Type (u+1)`-valued, REAL). `Scheme.HasRationalPoint C` = Nonempty section subtype of `C.hom`; the ONE genuine FGA sorry is `instHasPicScheme [HasRationalPoint C]`. The other three are typed-sorry carriers: `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType` (Kleiman §4 `th:main`(1), true under the rational point) and the pre-existing Quot-blocked `HasDivFunctor` / `HasAbelMap`.
- `instPicSharpRepresentable` proved by `choose_spec`; `groupSchemeStructure : CommGrpObj (PicScheme C)` proved via `CommGrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` + a `Multiplicative.ofAdd` NatIso (`picSharpCommGrpForgetIso`, naturality is `rfl` — no defeq walls anywhere in this file).
- Consumers: quantify over `[HasPicScheme C]` (synthesized from `[HasRationalPoint C]`) — `degree`/`kPoints_iff_kerDegree` in `IdentityComponent.lean` now carry it. `Pic0Scheme C := GroupScheme.IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)` is now a real def (axiom-clean), gated on `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType`.

**Caveat 1 — pointless curves:** `JacobianWitness` (`Jacobian.lean`) requires the abelian variety $J$ to exist UNconditionally, but route (b) only builds `PicScheme` for pointed $C$. For a genus-$\ge 1$ curve without a rational point the eventual `picardJacobianWitness` proof needs Galois/étale descent from a finite extension where $C$ gains a point (or a pointed restatement of the north star). Not blocking anything now — record before anyone tries to close `picardJacobianWitness` directly.

**Caveat 2 — `smoothProperQuotient` statement is WEAKER than Kleiman lm:qt and unprovable as stated:** Mathlib v4.31 has NO quasi-projectivity vocabulary, so hypothesis (2) of lm:qt is missing from the Lean statement; without it the statement is FALSE (Hironaka-type free ℤ/2 action on a smooth proper non-projective 3-fold: smooth proper equivalence relation, étale-quotient sheaf a non-scheme algebraic space). The former GLOBAL `instHasSmoothProperQuotient ⟨sorry⟩` (every presheaf representable!) was deleted this run (Ground-confirmed gone); the class is a use-site hypothesis only. Do NOT reintroduce a global instance, and do NOT try to prove the theorem as stated.
- **`pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (FBC leaf, `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean:186`) is NOT a cheap adjointness win — Ground's recommendation had the variance backwards. Route analysis (session 0013), do not re-derive.**

- The recommendation claimed "flat `g^*` is a left adjoint, so left-exactness follows from adjointness" — **false**: left adjoints preserve COLIMITS. Left-exactness of flat pullback is genuine flatness content, checked on stalks.
- No dodge exists at the presheaf level: `PresheafOfModules.pullback` is a bare `leftAdjoint` with no pointwise description; the sections-level formula `colim_{U ⊇ g(U')} M(U) ⊗_{O(U)} O(U')` is only RIGHT-exact (the ring maps are not flat — flatness lives on stalks). Sheafification + `SheafOfModules.forget` both preserve finite limits (Mathlib), so the irreducible core really is stalk-level.
- **Viable honest route** (est. 400–800 LOC of new infra): (1) combinators exist — `Functor.preservesFiniteLimits_tfae` / `preservesFiniteLimits_of_preservesKernels` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/ShortComplex/ExactFunctor.lean`); (2) Mathlib 2026 has module structure on stalks (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Stalk.lean`, `PresheafOfModules.germ_smul`) and stalk⊣skyscraper at plain-category level (`Topology/Sheaves/Skyscraper.lean`), plus `mono_iff_stalk_mono`/`isIso_iff_stalkFunctor_map_iso` (`Topology/Sheaves/Stalks.lean`); (3) the missing brick is **stalk-of-pullback**: `stalkModule_x ∘ pullback g ≅ extendScalars(stalkMap) ∘ stalkModule_{g x}` — provable by adjoint uniqueness IF a module-level skyscraper right adjoint is built (pushforward of skyscraper = skyscraper at the image); then flat stalk maps (`AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.iff_flat_stalkMap`-adjacent, `RingHom.Flat` appLE definition) + `ModuleCat.preservesFiniteLimits_extendScalars_of_flat` (`ModuleCat/Descent.lean`) finish via kernel-comparison-on-stalks.
- **Cheaper architectural alternative** if the instance stays stuck: the only consumer is `pullback_mapHC_homologyIso` at line 1545 (`cech_flatBaseChange`, affine S/S'), applied to the Čech complex whose terms are iso to tildes — a complex-specific homology-commutation via `tilde`-exactness (`TildeExactness.lean`) + `pullback_spec_tilde_iso` could replace the general instance, at the cost of a termwise-tilde chain-iso.
- **A.3 Pic0 cone state after run-0008 T5 — supersedes the run-0005 'IdentityComponent all 9 sorries blocked' memory.**

- **Proved sorry-free**: `Pic0.grpObj` (`Nonempty (GrpObj (Pic0Scheme C))` = `isSubgroupHomomorphism (PicScheme C)`; instances synthesize directly from `groupSchemeStructure` (CommGrpObj parent projection) + the `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType` projection instance; `Pic0Scheme` unfolds by rfl, plain `exact` works). Also `Pic0.geometricallyIrreducible` (specialisation of the substrate), both `isAbelianVariety` assemblies (the `Pic0Scheme`-namespace one MOVED to `Pic0AbelianVariety.lean`), and `tangentSpaceCotangentDual` now hypothesis-free.
- **Still blocked, do not re-probe**: `isFiniteTypeGeometricallyIrreducible` QC∧GeomIrred conjunct (EGA IV₂ 4.5.8/4.6.1 translate-cover argument, not in Mathlib v4.31); `degree`/`kPoints_iff_kerDegree` (Quot/Hilbert χ endgame); `finrank_eq_genus`, `Pic0.smooth`, `Pic0.proper` (need tangentSpaceIso resp. Chevalley–Rosenlicht).

  n=$(grep -nE '^\s*sorry\b|:=\s*sorry\b|by\s+sorry\b|\bexact\s+sorry\b|\bsorry\s*$' AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/$f.lean 2>/dev/null | wc -l)
  echo "$f.lean : $n"
done

6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus
8	
9	/-!
10	# The Albanese universal property of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` (A.4.d)
11	
12	This file is the **A.4.d** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
13	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages Milne's
14	*Abelian Varieties* §III.6 Proposition 6.1: for a complete nonsingular curve
15	`C` of genus `g > 0` over an algebraically closed field `k̄` and a marked
16	`k̄`-point `P₀ ∈ C(k̄)`, the Abel–Jacobi morphism
17	`ι_{P₀} : C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` is the **Albanese morphism** of the pointed curve
18	`(C, P₀)`: any pointed morphism `φ : C ⟶ A` into an abelian variety with
19	`φ(P₀) = η_A` factors uniquely as `φ = ψ ∘ ι_{P₀}` for a homomorphism of
20	group schemes `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`.
21	
22	The chapter commits to the **symmetric-power route** (cube-free): the
23	symmetric assignment `(P₁,…,P_g) ↦ φ(P₁) + ⋯ + φ(P_g)` factors through
24	`Sym^g C`, the resulting morphism `Sym^g φ : Sym^g C ⟶ A` descends through
25	the birational morphism `f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` to a rational map
26	`Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⇢ A`, and Milne's Theorem I.3.2 (`extend_to_av` in the sibling
27	`Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`, A.4.c) promotes it to a regular
28	morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`. The group-homomorphism property of `ψ` then
29	follows from `ψ(η_J) = η_A` together with Milne Corollary I.1.2 (a
30	consequence of `thm:rigidity_lemma`).
31	
32	## Status (iter-177 Lane 7 file-skeleton)
33	
34	This file is the **iter-177 Lane 7** file-skeleton. Each of the six
35	blueprint-pinned declarations carries the *intended* substantive type
36	signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
37	`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_AlbaneseUP.tex`) with a `sorry` body.
38	
39	The 6 pinned declarations are:
40	
41	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.abelJacobi` (noncomputable def, ~6 LOC) — the
42	   regular morphism `ι_{P₀} : C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` of
43	   `lem:abel_jacobi_morphism`. Encoded against the file-internal
44	   placeholder `Pic0.jacobianScheme` for `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` (the A.3 upstream
45	   has not yet been split out; see §0 below).
46	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.SymmetricPower` (noncomputable def, ~3 LOC) —
47	   the `g`-th symmetric power `Sym^g C` of the curve `C`, as a `k̄`-scheme.
48	   Body gated on iter-178+ `SymmetricPower.lean` substrate (Milne III.3
49	   Proposition 3.1; Mumford 1970 II.7 / III.11 affine-and-glue recipe).
50	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.symmetricPowerAVMap` (noncomputable def, ~5 LOC) —
51	   the symmetrised morphism `Sym^g φ : Sym^g C ⟶ A` produced by
52	   `lem:symmetric_product_av_map` from an input `φ : C ⟶ A`.
53	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.symmetricPowerToJacobian` (noncomputable def,
54	   ~5 LOC) — the birational morphism `f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` of
55	   `lem:symmetric_product_to_jacobian`.
56	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.descentThroughBirationalSigma` (theorem,
57	   ~10 LOC) — the descent of `Sym^g φ` to a regular morphism
58	   `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` via Milne Theorem 3.2.
59	6. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.albanese_universal_property` (theorem, ~12 LOC) —
60	   the headline UP theorem (Milne Proposition III.6.1).
61	
62	## File-internal placeholder for `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`
63	
64	The A.3 row of Route A (the identity-component refinement of the Picard
65	scheme together with its degree map) has not yet been split out into a
66	dedicated chapter or `.lean` file at the time of this file-skeleton
67	landing. We supply a **single helper** here: a `Pic0.Bundle` structure
68	bundling the underlying scheme together with its four abelian-variety
69	instances (`GrpObj`, `IsProper`, `Smooth`, `GeometricallyIrreducible`),
70	plus a typed-`sorry` carrier `Pic0.bundle C` and a derived definition
71	`Pic0.jacobianScheme C := (bundle C).scheme`. The four instances on
72	`jacobianScheme C` are derived from the bundle's fields (no extra
73	`sorry`). Once the A.3 chapter materialises this placeholder collapses to
74	a one-line re-export.
75	
76	Helper budget = 1: the single helper carrying a `sorry` is `Pic0.bundle`.
77	
78	## Note on type expressivity
79	
80	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
81	each pinned declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
82	
83	- `abelJacobi C P₀ : C ⟶ jacobianScheme C` — a genuine morphism into the
84	  Pic⁰ scheme (not `𝟙 C` or any tautology).
85	- `SymmetricPower C g : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` — a `k̄`-scheme, not `C`
86	  itself or `𝟙_`.
87	- `symmetricPowerAVMap C g φ : SymmetricPower C g ⟶ A` — a morphism into
88	  the abelian variety `A`.
89	- `symmetricPowerToJacobian C P₀ g : SymmetricPower C g ⟶ jacobianScheme C`
90	  — a morphism into Pic⁰.
91	- `descentThroughBirationalSigma` — `∃!` existence-and-uniqueness of
92	  `ψ : jacobianScheme C ⟶ A` satisfying
93	  `symmetricPowerAVMap C g φ = symmetricPowerToJacobian C P₀ g ≫ ψ`.
94	- `albanese_universal_property` — `∃!` existence-and-uniqueness of
95	  `ψ : jacobianScheme C ⟶ A` satisfying `φ = abelJacobi C P₀ ≫ ψ`.
96	
97	Unfolding any pinned declaration exposes the named substantive content
98	(a morphism into the Pic⁰ scheme, a unique factorisation through the
99	Abel–Jacobi map, …); no `Iso.refl _` / `Classical.choice ⟨witness⟩` /
100	empty-content `proof_wanted` placeholders are used.
101	
102	## Abelian-variety conventions
103	
104	Following the project (cf. `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`, `Jacobian.lean`),
105	an **abelian variety over `k̄`** is encoded as an object
106	`A : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying the four instances:
107	
108	- `[GrpObj A]` (group-object structure on the over-category),
109	- `[IsProper A.hom]` (complete),
110	- `[Smooth A.hom]` (nonsingular),
111	- `[GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]` (geometrically irreducible).
112	
113	A **smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over `k̄`** carries:
114	
115	- `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]`,
116	- `[IsProper C.hom]`,
117	- `[GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]`.
118	
119	These instances are supplied by `inferInstance` at the call site.
120	
121	## References
122	
123	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_AlbaneseUP.tex` (~830 LOC,
124	6 pins). Source: Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, §III.6 Proposition 6.1, p. 104;
125	§III.3 Proposition 3.1 (symmetric power), p. 94; §III.5 Theorem 5.1(a)
126	(birationality of `f^{(g)}`), p. 101 (`references/abelian-varieties.pdf`).
127	-/
128	
129	set_option autoImplicit false
130	
131	universe u
132	
133	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
134	
135	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
136	
137	namespace Pic0
138	
139	/-! ## §0. File-internal placeholder for `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`
140	
141	The A.3 row of Route A (identity component of the Picard scheme together
142	with its degree map) is not yet split out into a dedicated chapter / file.
143	The single helper below — a bundled structure carrying the Pic⁰ scheme
144	together with its four abelian-variety instances, plus a typed-`sorry`
145	carrier `bundle` — supplies the placeholder. Once the A.3 chapter
146	materialises this collapses to a one-line re-export.
147	
148	This is the **only** helper in the file that consumes a `sorry`. The
149	derived `jacobianScheme` def and the four typeclass instances below are
150	projections from `bundle` and do not introduce additional `sorry`s. -/
151	
152	/-- File-internal placeholder for `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`: bundles the underlying
153	`k̄`-scheme together with the four abelian-variety structural instances
154	(group object, proper, smooth, geometrically irreducible) supplied at the
155	A.3 row of Route A.
156	
157	Collapses to a re-export of the A.3 chapter once that materialises. -/
158	structure Bundle {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
159	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
160	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
161	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] where
162	  /-- The underlying scheme `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`. -/
163	  scheme : Over (Spec (.of kbar))
164	  /-- Group-object structure on `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`. -/
165	  grpObj : GrpObj scheme
166	  /-- Properness of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` over `k̄`. -/
167	  proper : IsProper scheme.hom
168	  /-- Smoothness of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` over `k̄`. -/
169	  smooth : Smooth scheme.hom
170	  /-- Geometric irreducibility of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` over `k̄`. -/
171	  geomIrred : GeometricallyIrreducible scheme.hom
172	
173	variable {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
174	
175	variable (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
176	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
177	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
178	
179	/-- File-internal **placeholder carrier** for `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` — a typed
180	`sorry` pending the A.3 row chapter. iter-178+: replace with the
181	`Bundle` produced by `Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean` (A.2.c) after
182	its identity-component / degree-map refinement (A.3) lands. -/
183	noncomputable def bundle : Bundle C := sorry
184	
185	/-- The underlying `k̄`-scheme `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` of a smooth proper
186	geometrically irreducible curve `C` over an algebraically closed field
187	`k̄`. Read off from the `Bundle` placeholder. -/
188	noncomputable def jacobianScheme : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) := (bundle C).scheme
189	
190	/-- Group-object structure on `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`. **Demoted from
191	`instance` per lean-auditor iter-196 must-fix**: the silent
192	propagation of `sorryAx` through `GrpObj`-typeclass synthesis is
193	a soundness exposure until the `bundle` carrier closes.
194	Consumers thread via `letI := jacobianScheme_grpObj C`. -/
195	noncomputable def jacobianScheme_grpObj : GrpObj (jacobianScheme C) :=
196	  (bundle C).grpObj
197	
198	/-- (Demoted from `instance`; see `jacobianScheme_grpObj`.) -/
199	theorem jacobianScheme_isProper : IsProper (jacobianScheme C).hom :=
200	  (bundle C).proper
201	
202	/-- (Demoted from `instance`; see `jacobianScheme_grpObj`.) -/
203	theorem jacobianScheme_smooth : Smooth (jacobianScheme C).hom :=
204	  (bundle C).smooth
205	
206	/-- (Demoted from `instance`; see `jacobianScheme_grpObj`.) -/
207	theorem jacobianScheme_geomIrred :
208	    GeometricallyIrreducible (jacobianScheme C).hom :=
209	  (bundle C).geomIrred
210	
211	/-! ## §1. The Abel–Jacobi morphism at a marked point
212	
213	The first sub-lemma packages the construction and elementary regularity
214	of the Abel–Jacobi morphism `ι_{P₀} : C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` at the basepoint
215	`P₀ ∈ C(k̄)`. On `k̄`-points,
216	`ι_{P₀}(Q) = [𝓞_C(Q − P₀)] ∈ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}(k̄)`; at the basepoint
217	itself `ι_{P₀}(P₀) = η_J`.
218	
219	This is the content gated on A.3 (existence of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` as a
220	representable group scheme); the morphism is the moduli classifier of
221	the rigidified diagonal correspondence
222	`𝓛^{P₀} = 𝓞_{C × C}(Δ − {P₀} × C − C × {P₀})` on `C × C`, taken as a
223	relative degree-zero line bundle on `C × C` over the second factor.
224	
225	Blueprint reference: `lem:abel_jacobi_morphism` (Milne, *Abelian
226	Varieties*, Proposition III.6.1 and Summary III.6.11). -/
227	
228	/-- **The Abel–Jacobi morphism at the marked basepoint `P₀`.**
229	
230	For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C` over an
231	algebraically closed field `k̄` and a marked `k̄`-point
232	`P₀ : 𝟙_ ⟶ C`, the **Abel–Jacobi morphism** is the unique regular
233	morphism `ι_{P₀} : C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` characterised on `T`-points by the
234	formula `(1 × ι_{P₀})^* 𝓜^{P₀} ≈ 𝓛^{P₀}`, where `𝓜^{P₀}` is the
235	Poincaré–Picard correspondence on `C × Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` and
236	`𝓛^{P₀} = 𝓞_{C × C}(Δ − {P₀} × C − C × {P₀})`.
237	
238	iter-178+: body constructed by applying the moduli interpretation of
239	`Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` (`def:pic_scheme` together with the identity-component
240	refinement A.3) to the relative degree-zero line bundle `𝓛^{P₀}` on
241	`C × C → C` along the second projection. Yoneda then produces the
242	unique classifying morphism `C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`. The basepoint
243	condition `P₀ ≫ abelJacobi C P₀ = η[Pic⁰_{C/k̄}]` (`lem:abel_jacobi_morphism`)
244	holds because restriction of `𝓛^{P₀}` to `C × {P₀}` is the trivial
245	line bundle `𝓞_C(P₀ − P₀) ≅ 𝓞_C` whose moduli class is `η_J`.
246	
247	For the iter-177 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
248	noncomputable def abelJacobi (P0 : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ C) :
249	    C ⟶ jacobianScheme C :=
250	  sorry
251	
252	/-! ## §2. The `g`-th symmetric power `Sym^g C`
253	
254	The `g`-th symmetric power of `C` is the quotient of the `g`-fold
255	Cartesian power `C^g` by the natural permutation action of the
256	symmetric group `S_g`. Mathlib `b80f227` has no formalised symmetric
257	power of a scheme — only `Sym` / `SymmetricPower` for types and
258	modules — so the scheme-theoretic construction must be supplied
259	project-side, following Milne's recipe of §III.3 Proposition 3.1: on
260	each open affine `U = Spec A ⊆ C` form `Spec(A^{⊗ g})^{S_g}`, then glue
261	along the standard open-cover patching (Mumford 1970 §II.7 / §III.11).
262	
263	This sub-build is a non-trivial dependency of A.4.d that the prover
264	must absorb before the body of `symmetricPowerAVMap` /
265	`symmetricPowerToJacobian` closes; the carrier here records the
266	substantive *target* `Sym^g C : Over (Spec k̄)` with a typed `sorry`
267	body, deferring the affine-and-glue construction to iter-178+
268	`Albanese/SymmetricPower.lean`.
269	
270	Blueprint reference: `def:symmetric_power_curve` (Milne, *Abelian
271	Varieties*, §III.3, Proposition 3.1, p. 94). -/
272	
273	/-- **The `g`-th symmetric power `Sym^g C`** of a complete nonsingular
274	curve `C` over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, as a `k̄`-scheme.
275	
276	Milne III.3 Proposition 3.1 constructs `Sym^g C` as the quotient of
277	`C^g` by the natural permutation action of the symmetric group `S_g`:
278	on each open affine `U = Spec A ⊆ C`, form `Spec(A^{⊗ g})^{S_g}`, then
279	glue along the standard open-cover patching. The construction comes
280	equipped with a canonical finite surjective symmetric morphism
281	`π : C^g ⟶ Sym^g C` characterised by the universal property that
282	every `S_g`-symmetric morphism `C^g ⟶ T` factors uniquely through `π`.
283	
284	This pin records the substantive *target* scheme `Sym^g C`; the
285	projection `π` and its universal property are recorded as helper API
286	in the iter-178+ `Albanese/SymmetricPower.lean` substrate file.
287	
288	iter-178+: body is the gluing-of-affines `Spec(A^{⊗ g})^{S_g}`
289	construction over an open affine cover of `C`. For the iter-177
290	file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`.
291	
292	The signature mentions `C` explicitly (not via the file's `variable`
293	block) so that the declaration's first explicit argument is the curve
294	`C`, matching the call sites `SymmetricPower C g`. -/
295	noncomputable def SymmetricPower
296	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
297	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
298	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
299	    (_g : ℕ) : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
300	  sorry
301	
302	/-! ## §3. The morphism `Sym^g φ : Sym^g C ⟶ A`
303	
304	The second sub-lemma is the elementary fact that the symmetric
305	assignment `(P₁,…,P_g) ↦ φ(P₁) + ⋯ + φ(P_g)` from `C^g` to `A`
306	descends through `π : C^g ⟶ Sym^g C`. The key point is that the
307	`g`-fold addition morphism `add_A : A^g ⟶ A` is itself `S_g`-symmetric
308	because the underlying group object `A` is commutative as a group
309	scheme; combined with the universal property of `Sym^g C`, this yields
310	the unique factorisation.
311	
312	Blueprint reference: `lem:symmetric_product_av_map` (Milne's proof of
313	III.6.1, p. 104, "Clearly this is symmetric, and so it factors through
314	`C^{(g)}`"). -/
315	
316	/-- **The symmetrised morphism `Sym^g φ : Sym^g C ⟶ A`.**
317	
318	Let `φ : C ⟶ A` be a morphism into an abelian variety `A` over `k̄`.
319	The composition `add_A ∘ φ^{×g} : C^g ⟶ A^g ⟶ A` sending
320	`(P₁,…,P_g) ↦ φ(P₁) + ⋯ + φ(P_g)` is `S_g`-equivariant (where `S_g`
321	permutes the factors of `C^g` and acts trivially on `A`), because
322	addition in a commutative group object is order-independent. By the
323	universal property of `Sym^g C` (`def:symmetric_power_curve`) it
324	factors uniquely as `Sym^g φ ∘ π = add_A ∘ φ^{×g}`.
325	
326	iter-178+: body is the universal-property invocation on the
327	symmetric composition `add_A ∘ φ^{×g}` once the iter-178+
328	`Albanese/SymmetricPower.lean` substrate exposes the projection `π`
329	and its UP. For the iter-177 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
330	noncomputable def symmetricPowerAVMap (g : ℕ)
331	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
332	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
333	    (_φ : C ⟶ A) :
334	    SymmetricPower C g ⟶ A :=
335	  sorry
336	
337	/-! ## §4. The birational morphism `Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`
338	
339	The third sub-lemma is the birational identification of `Sym^g C` with
340	`Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`. After choosing the basepoint `P₀ ∈ C(k̄)`, the cycle
341	map `(P₁,…,P_g) ↦ [𝓞_C(P₁ + ⋯ + P_g − g P₀)]` defines a regular
342	morphism `f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` which is birational by
343	Milne III Theorem 5.1(a) (the proof uses dimension count plus the
344	Riemann–Roch identification of the generic fibre as a single point on
345	a curve of genus `g`).
346	
347	Blueprint reference: `lem:symmetric_product_to_jacobian` (Milne,
348	*Abelian Varieties*, Theorem III.5.1(a), p. 101). -/
349	
350	/-- **The birational morphism `f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`.**
351	
352	Given a basepoint `P₀ : 𝟙_ ⟶ C`, the symmetrisation of the Abel–Jacobi
353	morphism `add_J ∘ ι_{P₀}^{×g} : C^g ⟶ J^g ⟶ J` descends through
354	`π : C^g ⟶ Sym^g C` to a regular morphism
355	`f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` sending an effective divisor class
356	`[P₁ + ⋯ + P_g]` to `[𝓞_C(P₁ + ⋯ + P_g − g P₀)] ∈ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}(k̄)`.
357	By Milne III Theorem 5.1(a) this morphism is *birational*: it is
358	dominant with one-dimensional fibres, and its generic fibre is a single
359	`k̄`-point (the linear system `|D|` of a general degree-`g` divisor `D`
360	on a curve of genus `g` is `0`-dimensional by Riemann–Roch).
361	
362	The birationality data (a dense open subset `U ⊆ Sym^g C` mapping
363	isomorphically onto a dense open `V ⊆ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`) is recorded as
364	helper API in the iter-178+ proof body of
365	`descentThroughBirationalSigma`.
366	
367	iter-178+: body is the specialisation of `symmetricPowerAVMap` to
368	`A := jacobianScheme C` and `φ := abelJacobi C P₀`. For the iter-177
369	file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
370	noncomputable def symmetricPowerToJacobian
371	    (_P0 : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ C) (g : ℕ) :
372	    SymmetricPower C g ⟶ jacobianScheme C :=
373	  sorry
374	
375	/-! ## §5. Descent through the birational quotient
376	
377	The final auxiliary lemma packages the two-step descent that promotes
378	`Sym^g φ : Sym^g C ⟶ A` (after passing through the birational morphism
379	`f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`) to a regular morphism
380	`ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`. The descent is a rational map
381	`Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⇢ A` on the dense open of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` on which
382	`f^{(g)}` is an isomorphism, then promoted to a regular morphism via
383	Milne's Theorem I.3.2 (the sibling `Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`).
384	This is the unique step of the proof that consumes A.4.c.
385	
386	Blueprint reference: `lem:descent_through_birational_sigma` (Milne's
387	proof of III.6.1, p. 104, "It therefore defines a rational map
388	`ψ : J → A`, which (I 3.2) shows to be a regular map"). -/
389	
390	/-- **Descent of `Sym^g φ` to a regular morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`.**
391	
392	There is a *unique* regular morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` such that
393	`symmetricPowerAVMap C (genus C) φ = symmetricPowerToJacobian C P₀ (genus C) ≫ ψ`
394	(equivalently, `ψ` is the unique regular extension of the rational map
395	`Sym^g φ ∘ (f^{(g)})^{-1} : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⇢ A`).
396	
397	The rational map is constructed on the dense open `V ⊆ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`
398	where `f^{(g)}` is an isomorphism, then promoted to a regular morphism
399	on all of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` via Milne's Theorem I.3.2 (sibling
400	`Scheme.RationalMap.extend_to_av` in
401	`Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`, A.4.c). Uniqueness is the
402	standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle: two regular
403	morphisms `Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` agreeing on a dense open are equal.
404	
405	iter-178+: body invokes `Scheme.RationalMap.extend_to_av` on the
406	rational map produced by inverting `f^{(g)}` on its dense open of
407	isomorphism. For the iter-177 file-skeleton the body is a typed
408	`sorry`. -/
409	theorem descentThroughBirationalSigma
410	    (P0 : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ C)
411	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
412	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
413	    (φ : C ⟶ A) (_hφ : P0 ≫ φ = η[A]) :
414	    ∃! (ψ : jacobianScheme C ⟶ A),
415	      symmetricPowerAVMap C (genus C) φ
416	        = symmetricPowerToJacobian C P0 (genus C) ≫ ψ := by
417	  sorry
418	
419	/-! ## §5.5. Albanese ⇔ symmetric-power equation (connecting biconditional)
420	
421	The iter-182 structural decomposition extracts the precise *connecting
422	identity* between the Albanese-form factorisation
423	`φ = abelJacobi C P₀ ≫ ψ` and the symmetric-power-form factorisation
424	`symmetricPowerAVMap C (genus C) φ = symmetricPowerToJacobian C P₀
425	(genus C) ≫ ψ` as a named typed-`sorry` biconditional helper. With this
426	biconditional in hand the body of `albanese_universal_property` reduces
427	to sorry-free assembly from `descentThroughBirationalSigma`. -/
428	
429	/-- **Connecting biconditional: Albanese-form equation ⇔ symmetric-power-form
430	equation.**
431	
432	For every candidate descent morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`, the Albanese-form
433	factorisation `φ = abelJacobi C P₀ ≫ ψ` holds iff the symmetric-power-form
434	factorisation `symmetricPowerAVMap C (genus C) φ = symmetricPowerToJacobian
435	C P₀ (genus C) ≫ ψ` holds. The forward direction precomposes both sides of
436	the Albanese equation with the symmetric `g`-fold sum, then descends through
437	`π : C^g ⟶ Sym^g C` and uses the additivity of `ψ` (a homomorphism of group
438	schemes). The reverse direction restricts the symmetric-power-form equation
439	along the diagonal embedding `Q ↦ (Q, P₀, …, P₀)` and uses
440	`φ(P₀) = η_A`.
441	
442	iter-200+: body uses the `Sym^g` projection π and its UP from
443	`Albanese/SymmetricPower.lean` (substrate file not yet opened),
444	together with the explicit Milne III §6 identification of `ι_{P₀}` with
445	the composite of `Q ↦ Q + (g - 1) P₀` followed by `f^{(g)}`. For the
446	iter-182 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry` — but the named
447	substantive helper exposes the precise connecting identity, allowing
448	`albanese_universal_property` to be assembled sorry-free from it. -/
449	theorem albanese_eq_iff_symmetricPower_eq
450	    (P0 : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ C)
451	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
452	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
453	    (φ : C ⟶ A) (_hφ : P0 ≫ φ = η[A]) :
454	    ∀ (ψ : jacobianScheme C ⟶ A),
455	      (φ = abelJacobi C P0 ≫ ψ) ↔
456	      (symmetricPowerAVMap C (genus C) φ
457	        = symmetricPowerToJacobian C P0 (genus C) ≫ ψ) :=
458	  sorry
459	
460	/-! ## §6. The Albanese universal property — headline theorem
461	
462	The headline result of the chapter: for every pointed morphism
463	`φ : C ⟶ A` into an abelian variety `A` with `φ(P₀) = η_A`, there
464	exists a unique regular morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` such that
465	`φ = ψ ∘ ι_{P₀}`. The proof follows Milne Proposition III.6.1
466	verbatim, using the four sub-lemmas above plus Milne Theorem I.3.2
467	(`extend_to_av`) and Corollary I.1.2 (a `thm:rigidity_lemma`
468	consequence).
469	
470	Blueprint reference: `thm:albanese_universal_property` (Milne,
471	*Abelian Varieties*, Proposition III.6.1, p. 104). -/
472	
473	/-- **Albanese universal property of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` — Milne III §6
474	Proposition 6.1.**
475	
476	For a complete nonsingular curve `C` of genus `g > 0` over an
477	algebraically closed field `k̄`, a marked `k̄`-point `P₀ ∈ C(k̄)`, and
478	every pointed morphism `φ : C ⟶ A` into an abelian variety `A` with
479	`φ(P₀) = η_A`, there exists a *unique* regular morphism
480	`ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` such that `φ = ι_{P₀} ≫ ψ`.
481	
482	Proof sketch (Milne III.6.1):
483	1. **Symmetrisation.** `(P₁,…,P_g) ↦ φ(P₁) + ⋯ + φ(P_g)` is
484	   `S_g`-symmetric (`A` commutative), so factors through
485	   `Sym^g C` as `Sym^g φ` (`lem:symmetric_product_av_map`).
486	2. **Descent to a regular morphism on `J`.** The birational morphism
487	   `f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` (`lem:symmetric_product_to_jacobian`)
488	   exhibits `Sym^g φ ∘ (f^{(g)})^{-1}` as a rational map
489	   `Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⇢ A`, which by Milne Theorem I.3.2 extends uniquely to
490	   a regular morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`
491	   (`lem:descent_through_birational_sigma`).
492	3. **Factorisation `ψ ∘ ι_{P₀} = φ`.** Use Milne's identification of
493	   `ι_{P₀}` with the composite `Q ↦ Q + (g − 1) P₀` followed by
494	   `f^{(g)}`; together with `φ(P₀) = η_A` and the additive structure
495	   of `A`, this gives `ψ(ι_{P₀}(Q)) = φ(Q)` on `k̄`-points, hence
496	   globally by the reduced-and-separated agreement principle.
497	4. **Homomorphism property.** `ψ` sends `η_J = ι_{P₀}(P₀)` to
498	   `φ(P₀) = η_A`, so by Milne Corollary I.1.2 (a `thm:rigidity_lemma`
499	   consequence) it is a homomorphism of group schemes.
500	5. **Uniqueness.** Two homomorphisms `Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` with the same
501	   restriction to `ι_{P₀}(C)` agree on the `g`-fold sum
502	   `ι_{P₀}(C) + ⋯ + ι_{P₀}(C) = Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` (since `f^{(g)}` is
503	   surjective), hence are equal.
504	
505	The iter-177 file-skeleton encodes only the existence-and-uniqueness
506	of the factoring morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` (matching the
507	project's `IsAlbanese` precedent in `Jacobian.lean`); the
508	group-homomorphism property is implicit in the construction. The body
509	consumes its four sub-lemma exports plus
510	`Scheme.RationalMap.extend_to_av` (A.4.c) and Milne Corollary I.1.2
511	(registered in `chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity`).
512	
513	iter-182 update: structural decomposition lands the body as
514	sorry-free assembly from `descentThroughBirationalSigma` (§5)
515	combined with the named typed-`sorry` biconditional helper
516	`albanese_eq_iff_symmetricPower_eq` (§5.5). The remaining substantive
517	gaps (substrate files `Pic⁰` representability, `Sym^g C` construction,
518	`extend_to_av` rational-map extension, and the connecting biconditional
519	body itself) remain blocking and are deferred to iter-200+. -/
520	theorem albanese_universal_property
521	    (_hg : 0 < genus C)
522	    (P0 : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ C)
523	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
524	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
525	    (φ : C ⟶ A) (_hφ : P0 ≫ φ = η[A]) :
526	    ∃! (ψ : jacobianScheme C ⟶ A), φ = abelJacobi C P0 ≫ ψ := by
527	  -- Structural decomposition (iter-182): reduce to the symmetric-power
528	  -- descent `descentThroughBirationalSigma` plus the connecting
529	  -- biconditional `albanese_eq_iff_symmetricPower_eq`. The remaining
530	  -- substantive gap is the biconditional helper (named typed-sorry); the
531	  -- proof body below is sorry-free assembly.
532	  have key := albanese_eq_iff_symmetricPower_eq C P0 φ _hφ
533	  obtain ⟨ψ, hψ_sym, huniq_sym⟩ := descentThroughBirationalSigma C P0 φ _hφ
534	  exact ⟨ψ, (key ψ).mpr hψ_sym,
535	    fun ψ' hψ' => huniq_sym ψ' ((key ψ').mp hψ')⟩
536	
537	end Pic0
538	
539	end AlgebraicGeometry

data.content:
=== lean-check skill ===
---
name: lean-check
description: Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors — use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
---

- During proof writing, prefer the Lean LSP MCP server for fast local feedback:
  inspect goals/diagnostics at the edited location before running a full build.
  This is the fastest loop for filling proofs.
- Treat LSP feedback as interactive guidance, not final verification. The
  faithful check is Lean's kernel via the project build: run the configured
  build command, `lake build <Module>`, or `lake env lean <file>` from the
  project root.
- Prefer the project's configured build command when validating a task. Otherwise
  use the narrowest faithful check that covers the changed file/module.
- Re-run after each change and repair failures you introduce.
- Read errors top-down — the first error often causes the cascade. For each,
  note the `file:line`, expected vs actual type, and remaining goal.
- A `sorry` left in place is an open obligation, not a success. Do not hide a
  hard obligation behind a new `sorry` unless a hint explicitly allows it.

## Fast LSP proving loop

The `lean-lsp` MCP server gives sub-second feedback — far faster than a build
(10–30s+). Drive proofs with it, then confirm with the kernel. Key tools:

- `lean_goal(file, line)` — the proof state at a line. Call before writing any
  tactic and after each one. Empty `goals_after` = that step closes the goal.
- `lean_diagnostic_messages(file)` — instant errors/warnings after every edit
  (`[]` = no errors, but *not* proof-complete — confirm with `lean_goal`).
- `lean_multi_attempt(file, line, snippets=[...])` — test 3–5 **single-line,
  indented** candidate tactics at once and see exactly which close the goal and
  why the others fail. The highest-leverage tool for filling a `sorry`.
- `lean_hover_info(file, line, col)` — type/signature/docs at an identifier
  (point at the first char). `lean_file_outline(file)` — declarations + line
  numbers without reading the whole file. `lean_run_code("#eval …")` — quick
  standalone `#check`/`#eval`/`#print`.

Core loop: `lean_goal` (what to prove) → search a premise (see the `leansearch`
skill: `lean_local_search` / `lean_leansearch`) → `lean_multi_attempt` (test
candidates) → edit with the winner → `lean_diagnostic_messages` (confirm) →
kernel-check the module at the end.

## Heavy builds — actually wait for them

A cold `lake build` (or one that recompiles Mathlib) can take many minutes. The
most common way a session ends on a wrong conclusion is **not waiting for the
build to finish**:

- Do **not** assume you will be "automatically notified" or "re-invoked" when a
  background build completes — whether that happens depends entirely on the
  mechanism you used. If unsure, run the build in the **foreground** and block on
  it until it exits, then read the exit code.
- If you run it in the background, you MUST actively wait: poll its status/output
  until there is a definitive exit code (a monitor / until-loop, re-reading the
  log), and only then conclude. A background job you started but walked away from
  is *not* done.
- Scope the build to the narrowest faithful check (`lake build <Module>` or
  `lake env lean <file>`) to keep the wait short; reserve the full project build
  for final validation.
- Never write your final report — or `complete` an inbox item, or mark a task
  done — while a build that affects your conclusion is still running. If the
  engine cannot wait long enough, say so explicitly under `## Issues` and leave
  the next action clear, rather than implying the proof checked.
- Before marking a task or roadmap item done/blocked/rejected, add a concise
  closing comment with the exact check command, result, relevant files, and any
  caveat. If a task has `roadmap_refs`, make sure the linked roadmap items end
  with a consistent status and their own sync/closing comment.

## When the LSP dies on big import files

The Lean LSP loads the full environment for the file you open. On a file whose
imports pull in most of Mathlib (a broad `import Mathlib` umbrella, or a module
that transitively imports one), that environment is huge and the LSP server can
run out of memory or time out and die. This is an LSP limitation, **not** a
proof error:

- Do not treat an LSP crash/timeout on such a file as a failure of your proof.
  Fall back to the kernel check: `lake build <Module>` or `lake env lean <file>`,
  which verifies faithfully without holding the whole environment interactively.
- Prefer opening the **narrowest** module in the LSP. Avoid pointing the LSP at
  umbrella/`import Mathlib`-style files just to get feedback; edit and inspect
  the specific module you are proving in instead.
- If the LSP has crashed, restart it (or just proceed with `lake build`) rather
  than repeatedly reopening the same giant file and getting the same crash.

############ project-git ############
---
name: project-git
description: How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
---

Read this before running `git diff`/`git log` on a project.

## The model

- **Workspace repo is out-of-tree too** — its git dir is
  `.archon-horizon/vcs/workspace.git` with the workspace **root** as its work tree
  (driven via `--git-dir`/`--work-tree`). **`<root>/.git`** is deliberately not touched, 
  because it is reserved for a user repo. It commits `config.yaml`, scoped project files, and `.archon-horizon/`
  shared state at integration boundaries; never the binary git dirs under
  `.archon-horizon/vcs/`, locks, or other volatile internals.
- **Project repos live out-of-tree.** Every project should have an out-of-tree
  git directory at `.archon-horizon/vcs/<project>.git`, with the project
  directory as its work tree. Project directories do not contain nested `.git/`.
- Expected cadence: both workspace and project repos should be commited at the end of each session, 
  but note that in the case of parallel runs, the commits order might not be sequential, 
  and some files might be modified by other runs, mostly for the workspace repo. However, 
  this should not be a problem in general. 

So a plain `git diff` at the root sees only a user repo (if any), never Archon's.
To inspect Archon's workspace or a project's history, drive its out-of-tree repo
explicitly.

```bash
# Workspace (manifest) history:
git --git-dir=.archon-horizon/vcs/workspace.git --work-tree=. log --oneline -n 20
```

## Reading a project's history / diff

Run from the workspace root:

```bash
GD=.archon-horizon/vcs/<project>.git
WT=<path-to-project>      # the project's `path` from the project list

git --git-dir=$GD --work-tree=$WT log --oneline -n 20
git --git-dir=$GD --work-tree=$WT diff HEAD~1
git --git-dir=$GD --work-tree=$WT diff HEAD~1 -- '*.lean'
git --git-dir=$GD --work-tree=$WT show <sha>
```

To see what the previous Horizon agent changed, diff from the checkpoint before
its session (use `log` to find the boundary).

## Caveats

- Every configured project is expected to have VCS enabled. If
  `.archon-horizon/vcs/<project>.git` is missing, report it as a workspace setup
  issue rather than treating it as normal, but work with it, it might be a new project, or a project that was cloned from the workspace's repo (hence without the vcs committed).
- You read history; you do not commit manually. Horizon checkpoints projects and
  the orchestrator writes the root integration commit.
- The bundled author identity is `Archon Horizon <archon-horizon@local>`.

data.content:
1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Albanese.CodimOneExtension
8	
9	/-!
10	# Milne Theorem 3.2: a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an abelian
11	variety extends (A.4.c)
12	
13	This file is the **A.4.c** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
14	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the single
15	theorem of Milne's *Abelian Varieties* §I.3, Theorem 3.2:
16	**a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an abelian variety extends,
17	uniquely, to a regular morphism**. This is the input that the Albanese
18	universal-property build (A.4.d, `Albanese/AlbaneseUP.lean`) consumes when
19	promoting the symmetric-product rational map
20	`C^{(g)} ⇢ A` to a regular morphism `J → A`.
21	
22	Milne's proof is two lines: combine Theorem 3.1 (the codim-$\geq 2$
23	indeterminacy result for rational maps from a normal variety to a complete
24	variety — project-side `thm:indeterminacy_codimGe2` of
25	`Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`, A.4.a) with Lemma 3.3 (the
26	pure-codim-$1$ indeterminacy structure for rational maps into a group variety
27	— project-side `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` of the same chapter). The
28	combination forces the indeterminacy locus of `f` to be simultaneously of
29	codimension `≥ 2` and pure codimension `1`, hence empty, so `f` is everywhere
30	defined.
31	
32	## Status (iter-175 Lane J file-skeleton)
33	
34	This file is the **iter-175 Lane J** file-skeleton: the single pinned
35	declaration carries the intended substantive type signature (matching the
36	blueprint `\lean{...}` pin in
37	`chapters/Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex` line 50) with a `sorry`
38	body. The body is iter-176+ work, gated on the sibling chapters
39	`Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` (A.4.a) and
40	`Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (A.4.b) landing their substantive
41	statements.
42	
43	The 1 pinned declaration is:
44	
45	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.extend_to_av` (theorem, ~10 LOC)
46	   — Milne Theorem 3.2: for `X` a smooth (= nonsingular) variety over an
47	   algebraically closed field `k̄`, `A` an abelian variety over `k̄`, and
48	   `f : X ⇢ A` a rational map, there exists a *unique* regular morphism
49	   `g : X ⟶ A` whose induced rational map equals `f`.
50	
51	## Note on type expressivity
52	
53	The signature is encoded against Mathlib's existing
54	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap` (a partial-map equivalence class on
55	dense opens) and `Scheme.Hom.toRationalMap` (the canonical "regular morphism
56	yields a rational map" coercion). The substantive claim is the existence
57	and uniqueness of a `g : X.left ⟶ A.left` with `g.toRationalMap = f`. The
58	type is non-tautological: the existence is the content of Milne 3.2;
59	uniqueness is the standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle
60	(`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant`).
61	
62	## Abelian-variety conventions
63	
64	Throughout the project (cf. `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`, `Jacobian.lean`),
65	an **abelian variety over `k̄`** is encoded as an object
66	`A : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying the four instances:
67	
68	- `[GrpObj A]` (group-object structure on the over-category),
69	- `[IsProper A.hom]` (complete),
70	- `[Smooth A.hom]` (nonsingular),
71	- `[GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]` (geometrically irreducible).
72	
73	A **nonsingular variety over `k̄`** (in the convention of
74	`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex` §1) is an
75	object `X : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying:
76	
77	- `[Smooth X.hom]`,
78	- `[GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]`,
79	- `[IsSeparated X.hom]`,
80	- `[LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]`,
81	- `[IsIntegral X.left]`,
82	- `[IsReduced X.left]`.
83	
84	These four-plus-two instances will normally be supplied by `inferInstance` at
85	the call site.
86	
87	## References
88	
89	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex`
90	(250 LOC, 1 pin). Source: Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, §I.3, Theorem 3.2,
91	p. 17 (`references/abelian-varieties.pdf`, PDF page 23).
92	-/
93	
94	set_option autoImplicit false
95	
96	universe u
97	
98	open CategoryTheory Limits
99	
100	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
101	
102	namespace Scheme
103	
104	namespace RationalMap
105	
106	/-! ## Milne Theorem 3.2 — rational maps to an abelian variety extend
107	
108	Let `k̄` be an algebraically closed field. Let `X` be a nonsingular variety
109	over `k̄` (smooth, geometrically irreducible, separated, locally of finite
110	type, integral, reduced). Let `A` be an abelian variety over `k̄`
111	(a group object that is proper, smooth, and geometrically irreducible). For
112	every rational map `f : X ⇢ A` there is a *unique* regular morphism
113	`g : X ⟶ A` whose induced rational map equals `f`.
114	
115	Milne's argument (`Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex` §1.4, Theorem 3.2):
116	let `U ⊆ X` be a maximal open of definition of `f` and `Z := X ∖ U`.
117	By the codim-$\geq 2$ indeterminacy theorem (Theorem 3.1, project-side
118	`thm:indeterminacy_codimGe2`) the closed subset `Z` has codimension `≥ 2`.
119	By the pure-codim-$1$ indeterminacy lemma for maps into a group variety
120	(Lemma 3.3, project-side `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy`), `Z` is either
121	empty or of pure codimension `1`. Both conditions force `Z = ∅`, so `U = X`
122	and `f` admits a regular representative on all of `X`. Uniqueness is the
123	standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle (cf.
124	`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant`): two regular morphisms `X ⟶ A` that
125	agree on a dense open of `X` agree everywhere.
126	
127	Blueprint reference: `thm:rational_map_to_av_extends` (Milne, *Abelian
128	Varieties*, Theorem 3.2, §I.3, p. 17). The pin in the chapter is at
129	`Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex` line 50.
130	
131	iter-176+ proof skeleton:
132	1. Pick a `PartialMap` representative `φ : X.PartialMap A` of `f` (using
133	   `Scheme.RationalMap.toPartialMap`, which exists for `X` reduced and `A`
134	   separated; both hold from the abelian-variety + variety instances).
135	2. Show `φ.domain = ⊤` by combining `thm:indeterminacy_codimGe2` (codim `≥ 2`
136	   complement) with `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (complement empty or
137	   pure codim `1`).
138	3. Read off `g := φ.hom` (the morphism on the full domain) and verify
139	   `g.toRationalMap = f`.
140	4. Uniqueness: two `g₁, g₂ : X ⟶ A` with `g₁.toRationalMap = g₂.toRationalMap`
141	   agree on a dense open of `X`, so by
142	   `AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant` and reducedness of `X` they agree
143	   everywhere. -/
144	
145	/-- **A scheme smooth over an algebraically closed field is reduced**
146	(Stacks `056S`/`033B` over `k̄`). Used to derive `IsReduced A.left` in
147	`av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred` below.
148	
149	**CLOSED (run 0002, T3 session 0021)** — no longer a typed sorry. The former
150	Mathlib gap is discharged by the project-side
151	`isReduced_of_smooth_of_isAlgClosed` in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`
152	(§3.B Step B.e): stalks are localisations of standard-smooth `k̄`-algebras,
153	whose maximal-ideal localisations are regular local (Step B.d′) hence domains
154	(`RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.isDomain_of_regularLocal`, Stacks `00NP` in
155	`AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean`), and reducedness is checked at maximal
156	localisations then transported to stalks and glued. The hypothesis is
157	accordingly strengthened from `[Field kbar]` to `[Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed
158	kbar]` (its unique consumer `av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred` already
159	assumes algebraic closure). Axiom-clean. -/
160	private theorem isReduced_of_smooth_over_field
161	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
162	    (A : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
163	    [Smooth A.hom] :
164	    IsReduced A.left :=
165	  isReduced_of_smooth_of_isAlgClosed A
166	
167	/-- **Iter-180 Lane G split (1/2): integrality of the abelian variety.**
168	
169	For an abelian variety `A` over an algebraically closed field `k̄` —
170	encoded in the project as `A : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying
171	`[GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]` —
172	the underlying scheme `A.left` is integral.
173	
174	**Derivation.** The proof has three steps:
175	
176	1. `Spec k̄` is a singleton scheme (Mathlib instance
177	   `Scheme.instUniqueCarrierCarrierCommRingCatSpecOf` provides `Unique`,
178	   which yields both `Subsingleton` and `Nonempty`).
179	2. `GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom + Spec k̄ a single point ⟹ IrreducibleSpace A.left`
180	   via Mathlib's `GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton`.
181	3. `IrreducibleSpace + IsReduced ⟹ IsIntegral` via Mathlib's
182	   `isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced`.
183	
184	The implication `Smooth A.hom ⟹ IsReduced A.left` — formerly the substantive
185	Mathlib gap of this helper — is now supplied project-side by
186	`isReduced_of_smooth_over_field` (backed by
187	`isReduced_of_smooth_of_isAlgClosed` in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`),
188	so this helper is **axiom-clean** as of run 0002, T3 session 0021.
189	
190	This was originally split out of the (since removed) iter-179 helper
191	`av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree`; it is a self-contained named lemma that
192	does not depend on the rational map `f`. -/
193	private theorem av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred
194	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
195	    (A : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
196	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
197	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] :
198	    IsIntegral A.left := by
199	  -- Step 1: `Spec k̄` has a unique point (Mathlib instance
200	  -- `Scheme.instUniqueCarrierCarrierCommRingCatSpecOf`); both
201	  -- `Subsingleton` and `Nonempty` follow.
202	  haveI : Subsingleton (Spec (.of kbar) : Scheme.{u}) := inferInstance
203	  haveI : Nonempty (Spec (.of kbar) : Scheme.{u}) := inferInstance
204	  -- Step 2: geometric irreducibility over a singleton base gives `IrreducibleSpace`.
205	  haveI : IrreducibleSpace A.left :=
206	    GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton A.hom
207	  -- Step 3: smoothness over the algebraically closed base field forces
208	  -- reducedness of the source (`isReduced_of_smooth_over_field`, closed in
209	  -- run 0002 session 0021 via `isReduced_of_smooth_of_isAlgClosed`).
210	  haveI : IsReduced A.left := isReduced_of_smooth_over_field A
211	  exact isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced A.left
212	
213	/-- **Milne 3.2, emptiness step: the indeterminacy locus of a rational map
214	to an abelian variety is empty.**
215	
216	For a rational map `f : X ⇢ A` of `k̄`-varieties from a nonsingular variety
217	`X` to an abelian variety `A` over an algebraically closed field `k̄`,
218	`indeterminacyLocus f = ∅`.
219	
220	**Derivation (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, proof of Theorem 3.2, §I.3,
221	p. 17).** Two project-side inputs combine:
222	
223	1. `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` (Milne Lemma 3.3 in
224	   `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`, the remaining `sorry` of this chain):
225	   for a rational map from a smooth integral variety to a *group* variety,
226	   the indeterminacy locus is either empty or pure codim 1 — every point of
227	   `Z(f)` specialises from a coheight-1 point *of `Z(f)`*.
228	2. `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete` (Milne Theorem 3.1 in
229	   `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`, proved axiom-clean): for a rational
230	   map from a nonsingular variety to a complete variety, every point of the
231	   indeterminacy locus has coheight `≥ 2`.
232	
233	If `Z(f)` were non-empty, (1)'s second disjunct would produce a point
234	`z ∈ Z(f)` with `coheight z = 1`, contradicting (2). Hence `Z(f) = ∅`.
235	
236	Both inputs require `f` to be a rational map *over `k̄`* (hypothesis
237	`hover`, Milne's ambient assumption), which the consumer threads through.
238	
239	**Refactor note (run-0006 T6, session 0015).** This replaces the former
240	`CodimOneFree`-valued helpers (`av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy`,
241	`av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree`, `codimOneFree_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`):
242	`CodimOneFree f` alone does NOT imply an extension for a complete target
243	(the removed `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` was false as stated — see
244	`existsUnique_hom_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`'s docstring), so the
245	Thm-3.2 chain now runs through the honest `Z(f) = ∅` intermediate. -/
246	private theorem av_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty
247	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
248	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
249	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
250	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
251	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
252	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
253	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
254	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
255	    (f : X.left.RationalMap A.left)
256	    (hover : f.compHom A.hom = X.hom.toRationalMap) :
257	    indeterminacyLocus f = ∅ := by
258	  -- Materialise the variety-package instances on `A.left` needed by Milne
259	  -- Lemma 3.3 and Theorem 3.1: `IsIntegral` from the split helper
260	  -- `av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred`; `IsReduced` from
261	  -- `isReduced_of_isIntegral`; `IsSeparated` from `IsProper`;
262	  -- `LocallyOfFiniteType` from `Smooth`.
263	  haveI : IsIntegral A.left := av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred A
264	  haveI : IsReduced A.left := inferInstance
265	  haveI : IsSeparated A.hom := inferInstance
266	  haveI : LocallyOfFiniteType A.hom := inferInstance
267	  -- Milne Lemma 3.3: the locus is empty, or every point of it specialises
268	  -- from a coheight-1 point of the locus.
269	  rcases indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme f hover with hempty | hpure
270	  · exact hempty
271	  · -- Milne 3.1 (codim-≥2) contradicts any coheight-1 point of the locus.
272	    rw [Set.eq_empty_iff_forall_notMem]
273	    intro x hx
274	    obtain ⟨z, hzZ, hz1, -⟩ := hpure x hx
275	    have h2 := indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete f hover z hzZ
276	    rw [hz1] at h2
277	    norm_num at h2
278	
279	/-- **Milne Theorem 3.2 — a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an
280	abelian variety extends, uniquely, to a regular morphism.**
281	
282	For `X` a nonsingular variety over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, and
283	`A` an abelian variety over `k̄`, every rational map `f : X ⇢ A` has a
284	*unique* regular extension `g : X ⟶ A` (i.e. `g.toRationalMap = f`).
285	
286	This is the input the Albanese universal-property build
287	(`Albanese/AlbaneseUP.lean`, A.4.d) consumes when promoting the
288	symmetric-product rational map `C^{(g)} ⇢ A` to a regular morphism
289	`J → A`.
290	
291	**Body (run-0006 T6, session 0015 refactor).** The proof is Milne's
292	two-line argument, run through the honest `Z(f) = ∅` intermediate:
293	
294	1. `av_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty` (above) combines Milne Lemma 3.3

=== the remaining CodimOne sorry context (line 1640-1680) ===
codim 1.**

For `X` a nonsingular variety over `k̄`, `G` a group variety over `k̄`
(a smooth group scheme of finite type, separated), and `f : X ⇢ G` a
rational map, the indeterminacy locus `Z(f) := indeterminacyLocus f` is
either empty (`f` is defined on all of `X`) or of *pure codimension 1*,
encoded as: every point of `Z(f)` lies in the closure of a codim-1 point
*of `Z(f)` itself*. (The `z ∈ indeterminacyLocus f` conjunct is essential:
without it the disjunct is nearly vacuous — on a variety every non-generic
point specialises from *some* codim-1 point — and, in particular, too weak
to combine with the codim-≥2 conclusion of Milne 3.1 to force `Z(f) = ∅`
in Theorem 3.2. Statement strengthened in run-0006 T6, session 0015.)

The proof (Milne's): consider the difference map
`Φ : X × X ⇢ G`, `(x, y) ↦ f(x) · f(y)⁻¹`. Then `Φ` is defined at `(x, x)`
iff `f` is defined at `x`. Pulling back the maximal ideal of `O_{G,e}` gives
a finite set of rational functions on `X × X` whose pole divisors are pure
codim-1; their intersection with the diagonal exhibits the indeterminacy
locus of `f` as pure codim-1.

Blueprint reference: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne, *Abelian
Varieties*, Lemma 3.3, §I.3, p. 17). -/
theorem indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
    {G : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
    [GrpObj G] [Smooth G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom]
    [IsSeparated G.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom]
    [IsIntegral G.left] [IsReduced G.left]
    (f : X.left.RationalMap G.left)
    (_hover : f.compHom G.hom = X.hom.toRationalMap) :
    indeterminacyLocus f = ∅ ∨
      ∀ x ∈ indeterminacyLocus f,
        ∃ z ∈ indeterminacyLocus f,
          Order.coheight z = 1 ∧ x ∈ closure ({z} : Set X.left) := by
  -- Milne's 4-substep proof body — see informal/milne-lemma-3.3.md for the
  -- detailed chain (TBD). Each substep is project-side buildable on top of
  -- Mathlib's Weil-divisor apparatus and the group-object API; substep 4
