  -- Mathlib's Weil-divisor apparatus and the group-object API; substep 4
  -- (pole-divisor intersection with the diagonal is pure codim-1) is the
  -- substantive Mathlib gap.
  --
  -- * Substep 1: construct the difference rational map
  --   `Φ := (id × inv) ∘ (f × f) ∘ m : X × X ⇢ G`, dense on `U × U` for
  --   any representative `(U, φ_U) ∈ f`. Needs the binary product on
  --   `GrpObj`-objects and the right-monoidal-cat structure used in this
  --   project (`MonoidalCategory.tensorObj`, `MonObj.mul`).
  -- * Substep 2: `(x, x) ∈ Dom(Φ) ↔ x ∈ Dom(f)` via the formula
  --   `f(x) = Φ(x, u) · f(u)` for any `u ∈ Dom(f)` in a chosen open
  --   neighbourhood — direct from the group-law continuity + openness of
  --   the domain of definition.
  -- * Substep 3: pullback of the local ring at the identity `e ∈ G`:
  --   `Φ` defined at `(x, x)` ⟺ `Φ^* (𝒪_{G,e}) ⊆ 𝒪_{X×X, (x,x)}`. Uses the
  --   `Scheme.RationalMap`-to-function-field machinery (the same gap that
  --   blocks `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction` at L744 below).
  -- * Substep 4: on the nonsingular variety `X × X`, the pole divisor
  --   `div(g)_∞` of any non-zero rational function `g ∈ Φ^*(𝒪_{G,e})` is
  --   pure codim-1; its intersection with the diagonal is pure codim-1 in
  --   the diagonal (Hartshorne AG 9.2, Krull's principal-ideal theorem).
  --   **Substep 4a (pole-divisor purity) is PROVED (run-0006 T6, session

/-! ## §6. Definedness at a prime-divisor generic point

A truthful, lightweight reformulation of definedness at a prime-divisor
generic point: `f` is defined at `W.point` iff `f` admits a `PartialMap`
representative whose domain contains `W.point`. This is a definitional
re-shuffle of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`
swapping the order of the two conjuncts under the existential for downstream
ergonomics (so the `toRationalMap = f` conjunct lands first, the way later
witnesses are constructed in this project).

### Status note (iter-179 Lane D, Path D2)

The blueprint pin `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction` (Hartshorne II.6
pp. 130–131) calls for the genuinely substantive statement

> `f` is defined at `W.point` iff every regular function on every affine
> open `V ⊆ Y` around `f(W.point)` pulls back to an element of
> `O_{X,W.point}` ⊂ `K(X)` of `ord_W`-value `≥ 0`,

which requires the pullback machinery
`Scheme.RationalMap → K(Y) → K(X)` and the project's existing
`Scheme.RationalMap.order : X.functionField → ℤ` valuation from
`RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean`. The Lean-level construction of the
`RationalMap`-to-function-field ring map is **not** shipped by Mathlib
at commit `b80f227`, and building it inside this file would constitute
a substantive new sub-build (cf. `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md`
on the pullback of a sheaf-of-algebras along a morphism).

iter-178's `extend_iff_order_nonneg` claimed the substantive statement
in its docstring but only proved this reshuffle; the lemma's name and
documentation are corrected here (auditor 178B's Path D2 honest fallback)
to match the proven content. The `[Ring.KrullDimLE 1]` and
`[IsLocallyNoetherian X.left]` typeclass binders that the substantive
statement would have required are dropped along with the renaming, since
the reshuffle does not use them. The substantive statement is deferred to
a future lemma (working title: `extend_iff_pullback_order_nonneg`) once
the pullback machinery lands.

Blueprint reference (in its current weakened form): a definitional unfold
of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`. -/

end RationalMap

end Scheme

end AlgebraicGeometry

data.content:
---
name: horizon-commit
description: Record your own work in the workspace ledger with semantic commits via `horizon commit` — small, meaningful, math-first messages; safe under concurrent runs.
---

Commit your work yourself, semantically, as you finish coherent pieces — don't
leave one giant undifferentiated snapshot for the system to sweep up. Good
commits are how the dashboard's change view and a human reviewer understand what
each session actually did.

## How

Use the Horizon CLI (never raw `git` on the ledger — the workspace repo is
out-of-tree with excludes, a secret guard, and provenance trailers that a bare
`git` would bypass):

- `"$HORIZON_BIN" commit -m "<message>" <files…>` — commit the files you changed.
  Paths are relative to your current directory (your project dir) or absolute.
- `"$HORIZON_BIN" commit -m "<message>" --changed` — commit everything you
  changed in the project (excludes the shared Horizon state, which the system
  commits separately).

Provenance (run / session / role / task / projects) is stamped automatically —
do **not** pass it. The commit is attributed to your role.

## Messages — math first

Write the message the way you'd tell a mathematician what you did: the result
and the idea, in human-readable prose with LaTeX where it helps; mention Lean
declaration names / files only as supporting detail. Mirror the house style,
e.g. `Prove H^i(X,F)=0 for i>dim X via the Čech-to-derived comparison`. One
coherent step per commit; split unrelated work into separate commits.

## Concurrency — you are not alone

Other runs may be committing to the **same** ledger at the same time. That's
fine and intended (it helps everyone converge faster):

- Commits are a single shared branch — they serialise and never conflict as a
  git *merge*; if `horizon commit` hits contention it already retried, so trust
  its result.
- Commit only the files **you** changed. On a shared worktree your commit can
  still capture another run's in-flight edit to the same file — that's expected
  and acceptable; the message + trailers still record your intent.
- Never `reset`, `rebase`, force-push, or otherwise rewrite the ledger. `horizon
  commit` only ever appends a commit.

## When

Commit at each coherent milestone (a lemma closed, a file completed), not only
at the end. Anything you don't commit is still captured by the system's
end-of-session sweep, but as an unlabelled blob — so commit it yourself to keep
the history legible.

435	/-- A rational map restricts to a map from `Spec K(X)`. -/
436	noncomputable
437	def RationalMap.fromFunctionField [IrreducibleSpace X] (f : X ⤏ Y) :
438	    Spec X.functionField ⟶ Y := by
439	  refine Quotient.lift PartialMap.fromFunctionField ?_ f
440	  intro f g ⟨W, hW, hWl, hWr, e⟩
441	  have : f.restrict W hW hWl = g.restrict W hW hWr := by
442	    ext1
443	    · rfl
444	    rw [e]; simp
445	  rw [← f.fromFunctionField_restrict hW hWl, this, g.fromFunctionField_restrict]
446	
447	@[simp]
448	lemma RationalMap.fromFunctionField_toRationalMap [IrreducibleSpace X] (f : X.PartialMap Y) :
449	    f.toRationalMap.fromFunctionField = f.fromFunctionField := rfl
450	
451	/--
452	Given `S`-schemes `X` and `Y` such that `Y` is locally of finite type and `X` is integral,
453	any `S`-morphism `Spec K(X) ⟶ Y` spreads out to a rational map from `X` to `Y`.
454	-/
455	noncomputable
456	def RationalMap.ofFunctionField [IsIntegral X] [LocallyOfFiniteType sY]
457	    (f : Spec X.functionField ⟶ Y) (h : f ≫ sY = X.fromSpecStalk _ ≫ sX) : X ⤏ Y :=
458	  (PartialMap.ofFromSpecStalk sX sY f h).toRationalMap
459	
460	lemma RationalMap.fromFunctionField_ofFunctionField [IsIntegral X] [LocallyOfFiniteType sY]
461	    (f : Spec X.functionField ⟶ Y) (h : f ≫ sY = X.fromSpecStalk _ ≫ sX) :
462	    (ofFunctionField sX sY f h).fromFunctionField = f :=
463	  PartialMap.fromSpecStalkOfMem_ofFromSpecStalk sX sY _ _
464	
465	lemma RationalMap.eq_of_fromFunctionField_eq [IsIntegral X] (f g : X.RationalMap Y)
466	    (H : f.fromFunctionField = g.fromFunctionField) : f = g := by
467	  obtain ⟨f, rfl⟩ := f.exists_rep
468	  obtain ⟨g, rfl⟩ := g.exists_rep
469	  refine PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mpr ?_
470	  exact PartialMap.equiv_of_fromSpecStalkOfMem_eq _ _ _ _ H
471	
472	set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
473	/--
474	Given `S`-schemes `X` and `Y` such that `Y` is locally of finite type and `X` is integral,
475	`S`-morphisms `Spec K(X) ⟶ Y` correspond bijectively to `S`-rational maps from `X` to `Y`.
476	-/
477	noncomputable
478	def RationalMap.equivFunctionField [IsIntegral X] [LocallyOfFiniteType sY] :
479	    { f : Spec X.functionField ⟶ Y // f ≫ sY = X.fromSpecStalk _ ≫ sX } ≃
480	      { f : X ⤏ Y // f.compHom sY = sX.toRationalMap } where
481	  toFun f := ⟨.ofFunctionField sX sY f f.2, PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mpr
482	      ⟨_, PartialMap.dense_domain _, le_rfl, le_top, by simp [PartialMap.ofFromSpecStalk_comp]⟩⟩
483	  invFun f := ⟨f.1.fromFunctionField, by
484	    obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := f
485	    obtain ⟨f, rfl⟩ := f.exists_rep
486	    simpa [fromFunctionField_toRationalMap] using! congr(RationalMap.fromFunctionField $hf)⟩
487	  left_inv f := Subtype.ext (RationalMap.fromFunctionField_ofFunctionField _ _ _ _)
488	  right_inv f := Subtype.ext (RationalMap.eq_of_fromFunctionField_eq
489	      (ofFunctionField sX sY f.1.fromFunctionField _) f
490	      (RationalMap.fromFunctionField_ofFunctionField _ _ _ _))
491	
492	/--
493	Given `S`-schemes `X` and `Y` such that `Y` is locally of finite type and `X` is integral,
494	`S`-morphisms `Spec K(X) ⟶ Y` correspond bijectively to `S`-rational maps from `X` to `Y`.
495	-/
496	noncomputable
497	def RationalMap.equivFunctionFieldOver [X.Over S] [Y.Over S] [IsIntegral X]
498	    [LocallyOfFiniteType (Y ↘ S)] :
499	    { f : Spec X.functionField ⟶ Y // f.IsOver S } ≃ { f : X ⤏ Y // f.IsOver S } :=
500	  ((Equiv.subtypeEquivProp (by simp only [Hom.isOver_iff]; rfl)).trans
501	    (RationalMap.equivFunctionField (X ↘ S) (Y ↘ S))).trans
502	      (Equiv.subtypeEquivProp (by ext f; rw [RationalMap.isOver_iff]))

export const meta = {
  name: 'albanese-endgame-unblock-map',
  description: 'Authoritative parallel investigation of exactly what is in-session-closable on the T9 Albanese endgame, plus blueprint consistency fixes',
  phases: [{ title: 'Investigate', detail: 'four independent read-only probes of the Albanese blockers' }],
}

phase('Investigate')

const FINDING_SCHEMA = {
  type: 'object',
  additionalProperties: false,
  properties: {
    area: { type: 'string', description: 'short name of the area investigated' },
    mathlibProvides: { type: 'string', description: 'exactly what Mathlib v4.31 already provides (declaration names, file paths)' },
    projectProvides: { type: 'string', description: 'what the AJC project already provides toward this (declaration names, files)' },
    gap: { type: 'string', description: 'the precise missing content' },
    minimalPath: { type: 'string', description: 'the concrete minimal step-by-step path to close the gap, with declaration names' },
    inSessionFeasible: { type: 'boolean', description: 'true iff a single focused session could plausibly produce a sorry-free, kernel-verifiable result' },
    locEstimate: { type: 'string', description: 'rough new-LOC estimate' },
    risks: { type: 'string', description: 'defeq/transparency/universe walls or other risks; cite project memory recipes if relevant' },
    concreteFixes: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' }, description: 'exact edits/fixes to apply (used mainly for the blueprint probe); empty if N/A' },
  },
  required: ['area', 'mathlibProvides', 'projectProvides', 'gap', 'minimalPath', 'inSessionFeasible', 'locEstimate', 'risks', 'concreteFixes'],
}

const ROOT = '/home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon'
const AJC = ROOT + '/MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge'

const probes = [
  {
    label: 'ff-bridge',
    prompt: `Read-only investigation. Determine EXACTLY how closable in one focused Lean session the "RationalMap -> function-field pullback bridge" is, for the Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge project (Lean v4.31, Mathlib in ${ROOT}/.lake-packages/mathlib).

Context: the file ${AJC}/AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean has ONE remaining sorry, 'indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme' (Milne Lemma 3.3, ~line 1662). Its substeps 1-3 need a way to pull a rational map f : X ⇢ Y (integral X) back to the function-field / stalk level, and the blueprint node 'thm:weil_divisor_obstruction' needs the same 'Scheme.RationalMap -> K(Y) -> K(X)' machinery. The project file ${AJC}/AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean already has Scheme.RationalMap.order : X.functionField -> Z and Scheme.Opens.functionFieldIso.

Mathlib provides (verify by reading ${ROOT}/.lake-packages/mathlib/Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Birational/RationalMap.lean): RationalMap.fromFunctionField : Spec X.functionField ⟶ Y (needs [IrreducibleSpace X]); RationalMap.equivFunctionField; also Scheme.Hom.stalkMap and generic-point / dominance API elsewhere in Mathlib.

data.content:
---
name: blueprint-conventions
description: The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
---

Blueprints are a LaTeX subset (the `leanblueprint` dialect) parsed into the DAG.
Write timeless mathematics — a standalone document, not a project journal.

## Environments

Each declaration is an amsthm-style environment; the recognized ones are
`theorem`, `lemma`, `proposition`, `corollary`, `definition`, `remark`,
`example`, `notation`, `convention`, and `proof`. A statement environment plus
its following `proof` environment form one node's content.

## Per-node macros

- `\label{id}` — a stable id (the DAG node key).
- `\lean{Name}` — the Lean declaration this node formalizes. Keep it 1-to-1: one
  blueprint node ↔ one Lean declaration, signatures matching.
- `\uses{a,b}` — dependencies on other nodes' labels; these drive the DAG edges.
  Add an edge instead of re-explaining a dependency in prose. Put `\uses` in the
  statement for definitional deps and in the `proof` for deps used only in the
  proof.
- `\source{slug:page-0001}` — reference anchors for UI popups and audit trails.
  Use page-level ids from `references/<slug>/tex/page-0001.tex` or an equivalent
  manifest entry. Multiple anchors may be comma-separated.
- `\leanok` — the Lean is written and checked. Honest only when its Lean is
  actually checked *and* its dependencies are too. On a `proof`, it means the
  proof is formalized; on a statement, the signature is formalized.
- `\mathlibok` — the declaration is already available in mathlib (no
  formalization needed; treat as a leaf).

## House style

- **Pure mathematics only.** No Lean tactics, typeclass/implementation notes, or
  semi-Lean pseudocode. No project history ("since iter N", "our failed route")
  and no conversational filler. If a Lean helper exists with no math counterpart,
  write its LaTeX statement so the 1-to-1 correspondence holds.
- **Complete proofs, not sketches.** A node's `proof` is a real, rigorous
  mathematical proof a reader could check — not a hand-wave or a TODO. The way to
  keep it short is to *split*, not to abbreviate: if a proof is long or a node
  grows past a short paragraph, break out the hard step as its own `\uses`-linked
  lemma (down to sentence-sized lemmas if needed) so each node stays small and
  its proof stays complete.
- **One declaration's worth per node.** Don't restate Lean source as prose.
- **Cite by source link, not quote comments.** Add `\source{...}` to the
  relevant node. Keep the retrieved/transcribed page text in `references/` (for
  example `references/<slug>/tex/page-0042.tex`) and let the UI expose it; do not
  paste verbatim source quotes into `% QUOTE` comments inside the blueprint.

data.content:
495	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
496	
497	open TopologicalSpace
498	
499	/-- **Pole-divisor purity.** Let `X` be a locally Noetherian integral scheme
500	all of whose stalks are regular local rings (e.g. `X` smooth over a field).
501	If `h ∈ K(X)` is not regular at a point `P` — i.e. `h` does not lie in the
502	image of `𝒪_{X,P} → K(X)` — then `h` is already non-regular at some
503	codimension-one point `z` specialising to `P`:
504	
505	`∃ z, z ⤳ P ∧ coheight z = 1 ∧ h ∉ im (𝒪_{X,z} → K(X))`.
506	
507	In particular the polar locus `{x | h ∉ im (𝒪_{X,x} → K(X))}` of a
508	function-field element is of *pure codimension one* in the "every point
509	specialises from a coheight-one point of the locus" sense — exactly the shape
510	of the Milne Lemma 3.3 disjunct (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`
511	in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`).
512	
513	Blueprint reference: `lem:pole_divisor_purity` (Hartshorne II.6.3A;
514	Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, §I.3 p. 17). -/
515	theorem Scheme.exists_specializes_coheight_eq_one_of_notMem_stalk_range
516	    (X : Scheme.{u}) [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
517	    (hreg : ∀ x : X, IsRegularLocalRing (X.presheaf.stalk x))
518	    (P : X) (h : X.functionField)
519	    (hP : h ∉ (algebraMap (X.presheaf.stalk P) X.functionField).range) :
520	    ∃ z : X, z ⤳ P ∧ Order.coheight z = 1 ∧
521	      h ∉ (algebraMap (X.presheaf.stalk z) X.functionField).range := by
522	  -- Choose an affine chart `U ∋ P` and set `R := Γ(X, U)`.
523	  obtain ⟨U, hU, hPU, -⟩ :=
524	    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := X) (x := P) (U := ⊤) (by trivial)
525	  haveI : Nonempty U := ⟨⟨P, hPU⟩⟩
526	  haveI hNoeth : IsNoetherianRing Γ(X, U) :=
527	    IsLocallyNoetherian.component_noetherian ⟨U, hU⟩
528	  haveI hFR : IsFractionRing Γ(X, U) X.functionField :=
529	    functionField_isFractionRing_of_isAffineOpen X U hU
530	  -- The stalk at `P` as the localization of `R` at `𝔭`.
531	  set p : PrimeSpectrum Γ(X, U) := hU.primeIdealOf ⟨P, hPU⟩ with hp_def
532	  letI : Algebra Γ(X, U) (X.presheaf.stalk P) :=
533	    TopCat.Presheaf.algebra_section_stalk X.presheaf ⟨P, hPU⟩
534	  haveI hlocP : IsLocalization.AtPrime (X.presheaf.stalk P) p.asIdeal :=
535	    hU.isLocalization_stalk ⟨P, hPU⟩
536	  haveI := functionField_isScalarTower X U ⟨P, hPU⟩
537	  -- Write `h = a / b` as a fraction of sections over `U`.
538	  obtain ⟨⟨a, b⟩, hmk⟩ := IsLocalization.mk'_surjective (nonZeroDivisors Γ(X, U)) h
539	  replace hmk : IsLocalization.mk' X.functionField a b = h := hmk
540	  have hb0 : (b : Γ(X, U)) ≠ 0 := nonZeroDivisors.ne_zero b.2
541	  have hdiv : h = algebraMap Γ(X, U) X.functionField a
542	      / algebraMap Γ(X, U) X.functionField b := by
543	    rw [← hmk, IsFractionRing.mk'_eq_div]
544	  -- Non-regularity at `P` says the denominator ideal sits inside `𝔭`.
545	  have hle : (Ideal.span ({(b : Γ(X, U))} : Set Γ(X, U))).colon {a} ≤ p.asIdeal := by
546	    have hnn := (div_mem_range_algebraMap_iff (K := X.functionField)
547	      (X.presheaf.stalk P) (q := p.asIdeal) hb0).not.mp (hdiv ▸ hP)
548	    exact not_not.mp hnn
549	  -- Every localization of `R` at a prime is regular: transport stalk
550	  -- regularity along the chart.
551	  have hregR : ∀ (q : Ideal Γ(X, U)) [q.IsPrime],
552	      IsRegularLocalRing (Localization.AtPrime q) := by
553	    intro q hq
554	    have hzU' : hU.fromSpec (⟨q, hq⟩ : PrimeSpectrum Γ(X, U)) ∈ U := by
555	      have h1 : hU.fromSpec (⟨q, hq⟩ : PrimeSpectrum Γ(X, U))
556	          ∈ Set.range hU.fromSpec.base := ⟨⟨q, hq⟩, rfl⟩
557	      rw [hU.range_fromSpec] at h1
558	      exact h1
559	    letI : Algebra Γ(X, U)
560	        (X.presheaf.stalk (hU.fromSpec (⟨q, hq⟩ : PrimeSpectrum Γ(X, U)))) :=
561	      TopCat.Presheaf.algebra_section_stalk X.presheaf ⟨_, hzU'⟩
562	    haveI hlocz : IsLocalization.AtPrime
563	        (X.presheaf.stalk (hU.fromSpec (⟨q, hq⟩ : PrimeSpectrum Γ(X, U)))) q :=
564	      hU.isLocalization_stalk' ⟨q, hq⟩ hzU'
565	    haveI := hreg (hU.fromSpec (⟨q, hq⟩ : PrimeSpectrum Γ(X, U)))
566	    exact IsRegularLocalRing.of_ringEquiv
567	      (IsLocalization.algEquiv q.primeCompl
568	        (X.presheaf.stalk (hU.fromSpec (⟨q, hq⟩ : PrimeSpectrum Γ(X, U))))
569	        (Localization.AtPrime q)).toRingEquiv
570	  -- Main ring theorem: a height-one prime `𝔮 ≤ 𝔭` containing the
571	  -- denominator ideal.
572	  obtain ⟨q, hqprime, hqht, hqle, hqcolon⟩ :=
573	    exists_height_one_prime_colon_le hregR hb0 (p := p.asIdeal) hle
574	  haveI := hqprime

55	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], Theorem~3.2, \S I.3, doc p.~17
56	  % (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF page 23).
57	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
58	  % "THEOREM 3.2. A rational map \(\alpha\colon V--\to A\) from a nonsingular
59	  %  variety to an abelian variety is defined on the whole of \(V\)."
60	  \textit{Source: Milne, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, Theorem~3.2, \S I.3, p.~17.}
61	
62	  Let \(X\) be a nonsingular variety over an algebraically closed field \(\bar k\), let \(A\) be
63	  an abelian variety over \(\bar k\), and let \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow A\) be a rational
64	  map. Then \(f\) is defined on the whole of \(X\): there is a unique regular morphism
65	  \(\widetilde f \colon X \to A\) whose restriction to some (equivalently, every) dense open
66	  set of definition \(U \subseteq X\) of \(f\) agrees with \(\varphi_U\).
67	\end{theorem}
68	
69	The uniqueness clause is immediate from \(X\) being reduced and separated: two regular
70	morphisms \(X \to A\) that agree on a dense open subset agree everywhere. The substance is
71	existence of the extension. Milne deduces it in one line by combining the codim-\(\geq 2\)
72	extension result for rational maps to a complete variety with the codim-1 indeterminacy
73	structure result for rational maps to a group variety:
74	
75	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
76	% "PROOF. Combine Theorem 3.1 with the next lemma."
77	\begin{proof}
78	  
79	  Milne's one-line proof is: \emph{combine Theorem~3.1 with Lemma~3.3}.
80	  Theorem~3.1 (the codim-\(\geq 2\) extension theorem for a rational map from a normal
81	  variety to a complete variety; valuative criterion of properness) is
82	  \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} of the present project; Lemma~3.3 (the
83	  pure-codimension-\(1\) structure of the indeterminacy locus of a rational map into a
84	  group variety) is \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} of the same chapter.
85	
86	  Concretely: let \(U \subseteq X\) be a maximal open set of definition of \(f\), and let
87	  \(Z := X \setminus U\) be its complement.
88	  \begin{enumerate}
89	    \item By \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} (Milne~3.1, the valuative-criterion extension
90	      to codim \(\geq 2\)), applied with the normal variety \(X\) and the complete variety
91	      \(A\), we have \(\mathrm{codim}_X(Z) \geq 2\).
92	    \item By \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} (Milne~3.3, the pure-codim-\(1\)
93	      indeterminacy structure for maps into the group variety \(A\)), the closed subset
94	      \(Z\) is either empty or of pure codimension \(1\) in \(X\).
95	    \item Both conditions together force \(Z = \emptyset\): a non-empty pure-codimension-\(1\)
96	      closed subset has codimension exactly \(1\), contradicting \(\mathrm{codim}_X(Z) \geq 2\).
97	  \end{enumerate}
98	  Therefore \(U = X\) and \(f\) admits a regular representative on all of \(X\), which is the

data.content:
=== leandag skill (how to query) ===
---
name: leandag
description: Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) — declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects — via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
---

The DAG links blueprint nodes by `\uses` edges. An edge `source -> target` means
*target depends on source*. The engine records each node's `\lean` link,
`proved`/`leanok` status, effort metrics, and Lean source metadata.

## Keep It Fresh

- Run the project build after Lean/blueprint edits so generated Lean artifacts and
  imported libraries are current.
- Run `horizon blueprint` after blueprint or Lean declaration changes; it writes
  `.archon-horizon/blueprints/<project>.json` for the dashboard and cached DAG
  consumers.
- Use `horizon leandag` for live inspection when deciding what to prove next.

## `horizon leandag` (preferred)

- `horizon leandag` — per-project summary: nodes, edges, proved count, and any
  isolated nodes (no edge in or out — usually a blueprint bug to fix).
- `horizon leandag -p <project> --node <label>` — one node's direct deps, full
  transitive cone, and reverse deps.
- `horizon leandag -p a -p b --union` — merge projects by declaration/node id.
  Same-id nodes are combined; divergent non-signature metadata is tolerated, but
  a Lean signature mismatch is an error to investigate.
- `horizon leandag -p a -p b --intersect` — only shared declaration/node ids,
  with the same signature check.
- Add `--json` for machine-readable output.

A node is "ready" when all dependencies are proved. Prefer ready nodes when
choosing what to attempt next.

## Cached JSON

`horizon blueprint` writes each project's DAG to
`.archon-horizon/blueprints/<project>.json` (`nodes`, `edges`, `dangling`) for
the dashboard. Under the leandag engine, `\uses` are resolved at build/cache
time, so `dangling` is empty there; the parser fallback still populates it. To
check for orphan/unresolved nodes, use `horizon leandag` rather than relying only
on `dangling`.

=== try querying node status ===
 Usage: horizon leandag [OPTIONS]                                               
                                                                                
 Inspect the blueprint dependency DAG: summaries, a node's cone, or the         
 union/intersection of several projects.                                        
                                                                                
╭─ Options ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ --project    -p      TEXT  Project to inspect. Repeat for several; default   │
│                            all.                                              │
│ --node               TEXT  Focus on one node (its deps, cone, rdeps).        │
│ --cone                     (with --node) emphasise the full cone.            │
│ --union                    Combine the selected projects into one DAG.       │
│ --intersect                Intersect the selected projects' DAGs.            │
│ --json                     Emit machine-readable JSON to stdout.             │
│ --help       -h            Show this message and exit.                       │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯

##### FULL RESULT 1 #####
AREA: RationalMap to function-field pullback bridge (germ O_Y,y to K(X); dominant K(Y) to K(X))
MATHLIB: Bridge is essentially a Mathlib one-liner. Stalk.lean:273 Scheme.stalkClosedPointTo (f:Spec R to X)[IsLocalRing R] : X.presheaf.stalk (f (closedPoint R)) to R := f.stalkMap (closedPoint R) then (stalkClosedPointIso R).hom (+ stalkClosedPointIso Stalk.lean:231, stalkClosedPointTo_comp, germ_stalkClosedPointTo, Spec_stalkClosedPointTo_fromSpecStalk, isLocalHom_stalkClosedPointTo). Birational/RationalMap.lean: Scheme.RationalMap.fromFunctionField [IrreducibleSpace X] (f:X rmap Y) : Spec X.functionField to Y (+ PartialMap.fromFunctionField, fromFunctionField_toRationalMap, equivFunctionField, eq_of_fromFunctionField_eq). Scheme.Hom.stalkMap (Scheme.lean:237) + stalkMap_comp + IsLocalHom (f.stalkMap x).hom. FunctionField.lean: Scheme.functionField := stalk (genericPoint X), Field X.functionField under [IsIntegral X], IsFractionRing (stalk x) functionField, genericPoint_eq_of_isOpenImmersion. Dominant: Scheme.RationalMap.IsDominant (Birational/Dominant.lean), IsDominant / Scheme.Hom.denseRange (Morphisms/UnderlyingMap.lean:217). Topology for base-point identity: genericPoint_spec, IsGenericPoint.image (Sober.lean:91), IsGenericPoint.eq [T0] (Sober.lean:79). Support: fromSpecStalk_closedPoint (Stalk.lean:101), Opens.fromSpecStalkOfMem_iota (Stalk.lean:172), TopCat.Presheaf.stalkCongr, Field to IsLocalRing (LocalRing/Basic.lean:122).
PROJECT: RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean: Scheme.Opens.functionFieldIso (lines 379-388 — the exact genericPoint-transport pattern the dominant lemma reuses), Scheme.RationalMap.order : X.functionField to Z plus order_zero/mul/inv, full PrimeDivisor API. CodimOneExtension.lean: private fromFunctionField_factor (line 1382) and heavy f.fromFunctionField use, incl. hff proving over-ness at the function-field level, in indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete (lines 1454-1526). CRUCIAL PRECEDENT: Scheme.stalkClosedPointTo is already used with a field R across Picard/TangentSpace{StalkAlgebra,SchemePoints,IdentitySection}.lean and GeometricallyConnectedSection.lean (e.g. stalkStructureHom_comp_stalkClosedPointTo, descResidueField_stalkClosedPointTo_fromSpecResidueField), so the field-stalk germ pullback is already battle-tested in this project.
GAP: No project name yet for the germ hom O_Y,y to K(X): it is literally Scheme.stalkClosedPointTo f.fromFunctionField (with y = f.fromFunctionField (closedPoint K(X))), buildable sorry-free as a near one-liner. The dominant refinement to K(Y) to K(X) needs ONE new lemma not packaged anywhere in Mathlib: f.fromFunctionField (closedPoint X.functionField) = genericPoint Y (i.e. the image of the unique point of Spec K(X) is Y's generic point when f is dominant and Y irreducible), then a stalkCongr transport of the codomain stalk from that point to genericPoint Y (= Y.functionField).
MINPATH: 1) def RationalMap.stalkPullback [IsIntegral X] (f : X rmap Y) : Y.presheaf.stalk (f.fromFunctionField (closedPoint X.functionField)) to X.functionField := Scheme.stalkClosedPointTo f.fromFunctionField — sorry-free 1-liner (IsLocalRing K(X) from Field via [IsIntegral X]). 2) lemma fromFunctionField_base_eq_genericPoint [IsIntegral X][IrreducibleSpace Y] (f)[f.IsDominant] : f.fromFunctionField (closedPoint X.functionField) = genericPoint Y — take representative g via f.exists_rep; identify the base image as g.hom(genericPoint g.domain) using Opens.fromSpecStalkOfMem_iota + fromSpecStalk_closedPoint + iota-injectivity + genericPoint_eq_of_isOpenImmersion (mirrors the functionFieldIso construction); then IsGenericPoint.image (genericPoint_spec g.domain) g.hom.continuous plus DenseRange g.hom (from f.IsDominant, giving closure(range)=univ) yields IsGenericPoint (image) univ; conclude via IsGenericPoint.eq against genericPoint_spec Y (schemes are T0+QuasiSober). 3) def RationalMap.functionFieldPullback [IsIntegral X][IrreducibleSpace Y] (f)[f.IsDominant] : Y.functionField to X.functionField := (Y.presheaf.stalkCongr (Inseparable.of_eq (fromFunctionField_base_eq_genericPoint f).symm)).hom then f.stalkPullback.
FEASIBLE: True | LOC: ~5 LOC for the germ def + ~40-70 LOC for the dominant base-point lemma and stalk transport = roughly 50-80 new LOC.
RISKS: CRITICAL / SKEPTICAL VERDICT: building this bridge closes ZERO existing sorries — it is pure infrastructure. (a) The sole CodimOneExtension.lean sorry, indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme (L1721), needs substeps 1 (difference map Phi:XxX rmap G via the GrpObj group law + product-of-rational-maps; also requires XxX integral, which is exactly why the theorem carries GeometricallyIrreducible), 2 (slice: (x,x) in Dom Phi iff x in Dom f), and 4b (diagonal codim-1 Krull bound) — all independent, substantial gaps. Substep 3 (this bridge) alone does NOT discharge the sorry. (b) thm:weil_divisor_obstruction (blueprint node 715) is UNPINNED: there is no Lean declaration or sorry for it; the only shipped Lean lemma is the fully-proved reshuffle mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point (node 717, no sorry). The bridge would only ENABLE writing a new extend_iff_pullback_order_nonneg, not close anything. (c) WeilDivisor.lean sorries L830 (support finiteness — gated by USER Route C PAUSE, needs [IsNoetherian X]) and L1138 (principal_degree_zero non-constant branch — needs phi:C to P1 + Hartshorne II.6.9 degree-multiplicativity) are unrelated to this germ bridge. INSTANCE/defeq risks: IsLocalRing X.functionField must resolve from the Field instance (holds under [IsIntegral X]); X.functionField is a bundled CommRingCat stalk (not CommRingCat.of T), but it matches the fromFunctionField codomain exactly, so no .of/coercion friction — precedent in Picard files confirms it works. Watch stalk-term unfolding past Scheme.presheaf poisoning keyed instances: use plain have (not haveI) for Field/IsDomain per the L1490-1494 recipe already in this file. stalkCongr orientation is a trivial .symm.

input.content:
/-
Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
Authors: Christian Merten
-/
import Mathlib

/-!
# Function-field pullback of a rational map

For a rational map `f : X ⤏ Y` out of an integral scheme `X`, Mathlib's
`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.fromFunctionField` gives a morphism
`Spec K(X) ⟶ Y` (the restriction of `f` to the generic point). Composing it with
`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.stalkClosedPointTo` produces the pullback of germs
`𝒪_{Y, f(η_X)} ⟶ K(X)` at the image of the generic point of `X`.

When `f` is **dominant** and `Y` is irreducible, that image point is the generic
point of `Y`, so the germ pullback becomes a field homomorphism
`K(Y) ⟶ K(X)` — the function-field functoriality `K(Y) → K(X)` that Milne's
rational-map extension leg (`Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`) and the
Weil-divisor obstruction (`thm:weil_divisor_obstruction`) both require.

## Main definitions

* `Scheme.RationalMap.stalkPullback` — the germ pullback
  `𝒪_{Y, f(η_X)} ⟶ K(X)` (no dominance needed).
* `Scheme.RationalMap.fromFunctionField_base_eq_genericPoint` — for a dominant
  rational map into an irreducible target, `f(η_X) = η_Y`.
* `Scheme.RationalMap.functionFieldPullback` — the induced field homomorphism
  `K(Y) ⟶ K(X)` for a dominant rational map.
-/

set_option autoImplicit false

universe u

open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace IsLocalRing

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

namespace Scheme

namespace RationalMap

variable {X Y : Scheme.{u}}

/-- **Germ pullback along a rational map.** For a rational map `f : X ⤏ Y` out of
an integral scheme `X`, the associated morphism `f.fromFunctionField : Spec K(X) ⟶ Y`
induces, by `Scheme.stalkClosedPointTo`, a local ring homomorphism from the germ
of `Y` at the image `f(η_X)` of the generic point of `X` into the function field
`K(X)`. -/
noncomputable def stalkPullback [IsIntegral X] (f : X.RationalMap Y) :
    Y.presheaf.stalk (f.fromFunctionField (closedPoint X.functionField)) ⟶ X.functionField :=
  Scheme.stalkClosedPointTo f.fromFunctionField

end RationalMap

end Scheme

end AlgebraicGeometry

  % on an open subset \(U\) of \(V\) whose complement \(V \smallsetminus U\) has codimension
  % \(\geq 2\).}"
  \textit{Source: Milne, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, Theorem~3.1, \S I.3, p.~16.}
  Let \(X\) be a nonsingular variety over \(\bar k\), let \(Y\) be a complete variety over
  \(\bar k\), and let \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) be a rational map. Then the
  indeterminacy locus \(Z(f)\) (\cref{def:indeterminacy_locus}) has codimension
  \(\geq 2\) in \(X\); equivalently, \(f\) is codim-\(1\)-indeterminacy-free
  (\cref{def:codim_one_indeterminacy}).

  Specialising further: if in addition \(f\) \emph{is} codim-\(1\)-indeterminacy-free
  ``for free'' (i.e.\ no prime divisor of \(X\) is contained in \(Z(f)\)), then
  \(\mathrm{Dom}(f) = X\) and \(f\) extends to a regular morphism \(\widetilde f \colon X
  \to Y\). The extension is unique because \(X\) is reduced and \(\mathrm{Dom}(f)\) is dense
  in \(X\).
\end{theorem}

=== Sober.lean 76-95 (IsGenericPoint.inseparable/eq/image) ===
protected theorem inseparable (h : IsGenericPoint x S) (h' : IsGenericPoint y S) :
    Inseparable x y :=
  (h.specializes h'.mem).antisymm (h'.specializes h.mem)

/-- In a T₀ space, each set has at most one generic point. -/
protected theorem eq [T0Space α] (h : IsGenericPoint x S) (h' : IsGenericPoint y S) : x = y :=
  (h.inseparable h').eq

theorem mem_open_set_iff (h : IsGenericPoint x S) (hU : IsOpen U) : x ∈ U ↔ (S ∩ U).Nonempty :=
  ⟨fun h' => ⟨x, h.mem, h'⟩, fun ⟨_y, hyS, hyU⟩ => (h.specializes hyS).mem_open hU hyU⟩

theorem disjoint_iff (h : IsGenericPoint x S) (hU : IsOpen U) : Disjoint S U ↔ x ∉ U := by
  rw [h.mem_open_set_iff hU, ← not_disjoint_iff_nonempty_inter, Classical.not_not]

theorem mem_closed_set_iff (h : IsGenericPoint x S) (hZ : IsClosed Z) : x ∈ Z ↔ S ⊆ Z := by
  rw [← h.def, hZ.closure_subset_iff, singleton_subset_iff]

protected theorem image (h : IsGenericPoint x S) {f : α → β} (hf : Continuous f) :
    IsGenericPoint (f x) (closure (f '' S)) := by
  rw [isGenericPoint_def, ← h.def, ← image_singleton, closure_image_closure hf]

end IsGenericPoint

noncomputable def stalkPullback [IsIntegral X] (f : X.RationalMap Y) :
    Y.presheaf.stalk (f.fromFunctionField (closedPoint X.functionField)) ⟶ X.functionField :=
  Scheme.stalkClosedPointTo f.fromFunctionField

end RationalMap
input.new_string:
noncomputable def stalkPullback [IsIntegral X] (f : X.RationalMap Y) :
    Y.presheaf.stalk (f.fromFunctionField (closedPoint X.functionField)) ⟶ X.functionField :=
  Scheme.stalkClosedPointTo f.fromFunctionField

/-- For a **dominant** rational map `f : X ⤏ Y` from an integral scheme into an
irreducible target, the image `f(η_X)` of the generic point of `X` under
`f.fromFunctionField` is the generic point of `Y`. -/
lemma fromFunctionField_base_eq_genericPoint [IsIntegral X] [IrreducibleSpace Y]
    (f : X.RationalMap Y) [f.IsDominant] :
    f.fromFunctionField (closedPoint X.functionField) = genericPoint Y := by
  obtain ⟨g, rfl⟩ := f.exists_rep
  have hdom : IsDominant g.hom := (PartialMap.isDominant_toRationalMap_iff g).mp ‹_›
  rw [RationalMap.fromFunctionField_toRationalMap]
  -- `g.fromFunctionField = g.domain.fromSpecStalkOfMem (genericPoint X) hx ≫ g.hom`.
  set hx : genericPoint X ∈ g.domain :=
    ((genericPoint_specializes _).mem_open g.domain.2 g.dense_domain.nonempty.choose_spec) with hx_def
  show (g.domain.fromSpecStalkOfMem (genericPoint X) hx ≫ g.hom).base _ = _
  -- the source point maps to the generic point of the domain `⟨genericPoint X, hx⟩`.
  have hP : (g.domain.fromSpecStalkOfMem (genericPoint X) hx).base
      (closedPoint X.functionField) = (⟨genericPoint X, hx⟩ : g.domain) := by
    apply g.domain.ι.isOpenEmbedding.injective
    have := congrArg (fun m => Scheme.Hom.base m (closedPoint X.functionField))
      (Opens.fromSpecStalkOfMem_ι g.domain (genericPoint X) hx)
    simpa using this
  -- the domain's generic point is `⟨genericPoint X, hx⟩`.
  have hgen : IsGenericPoint (⟨genericPoint X, hx⟩ : g.domain) Set.univ := by
    rw [isGenericPoint_def, ← Set.image_univ, ← genericPoint_spec (α := X.carrier),
      ← Set.image_univ]
    sorry
  -- push through `g.hom` (continuous + dense range) to get a generic point of `Y`.
  have himg : IsGenericPoint (g.hom.base (⟨genericPoint X, hx⟩ : g.domain)) Set.univ := by
    have := hgen.image g.hom.continuous
    rwa [Set.image_univ, hdom.denseRange.closure_range] at this
  simp only [Scheme.Hom.comp_base, TopCat.comp_app, hP]
  exact himg.eq (genericPoint_spec Y.carrier)

/-- **Function-field pullback of a dominant rational map.** A dominant rational map
`f : X ⤏ Y` from an integral scheme into an irreducible scheme induces a
homomorphism of function fields `K(Y) ⟶ K(X)`, obtained by transporting the germ
pullback `stalkPullback` along `f(η_X) = η_Y`. -/
noncomputable def functionFieldPullback [IsIntegral X] [IrreducibleSpace Y]
    (f : X.RationalMap Y) [f.IsDominant] :
    Y.functionField ⟶ X.functionField :=
  (Y.presheaf.stalkCongr (Inseparable.of_eq
    (fromFunctionField_base_eq_genericPoint f).symm)).hom ≫ f.stalkPullback

end RationalMap

theorem genericPoint_eq_of_isOpenImmersion {X Y : Scheme} (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f]
    [hX : IrreducibleSpace X] [IrreducibleSpace Y] :
    f (genericPoint X) = genericPoint Y := by

    Y.presheaf.stalk (f.fromFunctionField (closedPoint X.functionField)) ⟶ X.functionField :=
  Scheme.stalkClosedPointTo f.fromFunctionField

/-- For a **dominant** rational map `f : X ⤏ Y` from an integral scheme into an
irreducible target, the image `f(η_X)` of the generic point of `X` under
`f.fromFunctionField` is the generic point of `Y`. -/
lemma fromFunctionField_base_eq_genericPoint [IsIntegral X] [IrreducibleSpace Y]
    (f : X.RationalMap Y) [f.IsDominant] :
    f.fromFunctionField (closedPoint X.functionField) = genericPoint Y := by
  obtain ⟨g, rfl⟩ := f.exists_rep
  have hdom : IsDominant g.hom := (PartialMap.isDominant_toRationalMap_iff g).mp ‹_›
  rw [RationalMap.fromFunctionField_toRationalMap]
  -- `g.fromFunctionField = g.domain.fromSpecStalkOfMem (genericPoint X) hx ≫ g.hom`.
  set hx : genericPoint X ∈ g.domain :=
    ((genericPoint_specializes _).mem_open g.domain.2 g.dense_domain.nonempty.choose_spec) with hx_def
  show (g.domain.fromSpecStalkOfMem (genericPoint X) hx ≫ g.hom).base _ = _
  -- the source point maps to the generic point of the domain `⟨genericPoint X, hx⟩`.
  have hP : (g.domain.fromSpecStalkOfMem (genericPoint X) hx).base
      (closedPoint X.functionField) = (⟨genericPoint X, hx⟩ : g.domain) := by
    apply g.domain.ι.isOpenEmbedding.injective
    have := congrArg (fun m => Scheme.Hom.base m (closedPoint X.functionField))
      (Opens.fromSpecStalkOfMem_ι g.domain (genericPoint X) hx)
    simpa using this
  -- the domain's generic point is `⟨genericPoint X, hx⟩`.
  have hgen : IsGenericPoint (⟨genericPoint X, hx⟩ : g.domain) Set.univ := by
    rw [isGenericPoint_def, ← Set.image_univ, ← genericPoint_spec (α := X.carrier),
      ← Set.image_univ]
    sorry
  -- push through `g.hom` (continuous + dense range) to get a generic point of `Y`.
  have himg : IsGenericPoint (g.hom.base (⟨genericPoint X, hx⟩ : g.domain)) Set.univ := by
    have := hgen.image g.hom.continuous
    rwa [Set.image_univ, hdom.denseRange.closure_range] at this
  simp only [Scheme.Hom.comp_base, TopCat.comp_app, hP]
  exact himg.eq (genericPoint_spec Y.carrier)

/-- **Function-field pullback of a dominant rational map.** A dominant rational map
`f : X ⤏ Y` from an integral scheme into an irreducible scheme induces a
homomorphism of function fields `K(Y) ⟶ K(X)`, obtained by transporting the germ
pullback `stalkPullback` along `f(η_X) = η_Y`. -/
noncomputable def functionFieldPullback [IsIntegral X] [IrreducibleSpace Y]
    (f : X.RationalMap Y) [f.IsDominant] :
    Y.functionField ⟶ X.functionField :=
  (Y.presheaf.stalkCongr (Inseparable.of_eq
    (fromFunctionField_base_eq_genericPoint f).symm)).hom ≫ f.stalkPullback

input.new_string:
noncomputable def stalkPullback [IsIntegral X] (f : X.RationalMap Y) :
    Y.presheaf.stalk (f.fromFunctionField (closedPoint X.functionField)) ⟶ X.functionField :=
  Scheme.stalkClosedPointTo f.fromFunctionField

/-!
## Dominant refinement (planned)

When `f` is **dominant** and `Y` is irreducible, the base point
`f.fromFunctionField (closedPoint K(X))` equals `genericPoint Y`, so `stalkPullback`
becomes a field homomorphism `K(Y) ⟶ K(X)`:

* `fromFunctionField_base_eq_genericPoint`: reduce to a representative `g` via
  `RationalMap.exists_rep` (`hdom : IsDominant g.hom` from
  `PartialMap.isDominant_toRationalMap_iff`); the composite
  `g.fromFunctionField = g.domain.fromSpecStalkOfMem (genericPoint X) hx ≫ g.hom`
  sends `closedPoint` to `genericPoint g.domain.toScheme` (identify the inner
  point via `Opens.fromSpecStalkOfMem_ι` + `Scheme.fromSpecStalk_closedPoint`,
  `g.domain.ι` injective; the domain's generic point via
  `genericPoint_eq_of_isOpenImmersion g.domain.ι`); then
  `(genericPoint_spec _).image g.hom.continuous` with `hdom.denseRange.closure_range`
  and `IsGenericPoint.eq` (schemes are T0) give `= genericPoint Y`.
* `functionFieldPullback := (Y.presheaf.stalkCongr (Inseparable.of_eq …)).hom ≫ f.stalkPullback`.

Two support obligations block a one-shot close and are the concrete remaining
work: `IrreducibleSpace g.domain.toScheme` (dense open of an integral scheme —
does not auto-synthesise; prove `Nonempty` from `dense_domain` and
`PreirreducibleSpace` from the ambient irreducibility) and the `↥g.domain` vs
`↥↑g.domain` Opens-carrier coercion when feeding `⟨genericPoint X, hx⟩` to
`g.hom`.
-/

end RationalMap

input.content:
---
name: t9-albanese-endgame-unblock-map
description: T9 Albanese endgame after the port landed — the two greenfield sub-builds that gate it, the sole extension-leg soundness sorry, the function-field germ-pullback bridge, and blueprint fixes (run 0013 T9)
metadata:
  type: project
---

**T9 Albanese endgame: the rational-map-extension port LANDED + verified; both remaining legs are gated on large greenfield sub-builds, not one-session work. (run 0013 T9, session 0017.)**

- **Port done + verified.** All 8 `AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/*.lean` files are in AJC, byte-identical to the `Albanese` subproject, wired in the root, and `lake build` green (8573 jobs). CodimOne 3→1, Thm32 2→0 in-file sorries. Support files `PolePurity`/`SmoothPrimeRegularity`/`StandardSmoothDimension` are **axiom-clean** (kernel-verified `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`). The port was already captured in the run-0013 baseline snapshot `3029a02d` — `horizon commit` on it is a no-op (nothing to commit). So [[pullback-tilde-01hq-closed]]-style "port before assembling" (inbox I-0112) is now COMPLETE.

- **Extension leg soundness = ONE sorry.** `extend_to_av` (Thm32, sorry-free in-file) and `albanese_universal_property` (a sorry-free *assembly*) both carry `sorryAx` transitively via the single sorry `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` (Milne Lemma 3.3, `CodimOneExtension.lean:1662`). It needs 4 substeps: (1) difference map `Φ:X×X⇢G` via the `GrpObj` group law + product-of-rational-maps (needs X×X integral — why the thm carries `GeometricallyIrreducible`); (2) slice `(x,x)∈Dom Φ ↔ x∈Dom f`; (3) function-field pullback; (4b) diagonal codim-1 Krull bound (4a already closed in `PolePurity.lean`). Substeps 1,2,4b are independent greenfield gaps — NOT one session. Do not expect the bridge below to close this.

- **UP-assembly leg blocked on the symmetric-power scheme substrate.** Mathlib v4.31 has **no** scheme-level `Sym^g`, no quotient of a scheme by a finite group action, no GIT/geometric quotient, no Noether finiteness of invariants (only type/module `Sym`, `FixedPoints.subalgebra/subring/subfield`, `Algebra.IsInvariant` for Galois). All of `AlbaneseUP.lean`'s `abelJacobi`/`SymmetricPower`/`symmetricPowerAVMap`/`symmetricPowerToJacobian`/`descentThroughBirationalSigma`/`albanese_eq_iff_symmetricPower_eq` are downstream. Building `Sym^g C` (Milne III.3, glue `Spec((A^{⊗g})^{S_g})`) is a large multi-session sub-build. `bundle` (A.3 representability) is out of T9 scope. `albanese_universal_property` is ALREADY sorry-free assembly from the two theorem sorries.

- **Bridge substrate landed (infrastructure, closes NO sorry).** `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.stalkPullback` (`Albanese/RationalMapFunctionField.lean`, axiom-clean) = the germ pullback `𝒪_{Y,f(η_X)} ⟶ K(X)` via `Scheme.stalkClosedPointTo f.fromFunctionField` — the `RationalMap→K(Y)→K(X)` functoriality the project lacked. The **dominant refinement** to a field hom `K(Y)⟶K(X)` (`fromFunctionField_base_eq_genericPoint` + `functionFieldPullback`) is documented in-file with the full recipe; it walls on two obligations: `IrreducibleSpace g.domain.toScheme` (dense open of integral — does NOT auto-synthesise) and the `↥g.domain` vs `↥↑g.domain` Opens-carrier coercion feeding `g.hom`. Skeptical note: `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction` is UNPINNED (no Lean decl/sorry), so even the field hom closes nothing directly — it only *enables* a future `extend_iff_pullback_order_nonneg`.

- **Blueprint fixes applied (`e9e950951a`).** Thm32 chapter: three helper nodes pinning the deleted CodimOneFree-route decls → one honest `lem:av_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty` node. CodimOne chapter: `thm:codim_one_extension` re-pinned from the removed, false-as-stated `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` to `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete`, and its false "CodimOneFree ⇒ extension" paragraph deleted (the ℙ²⇢ℙ¹ hazard — never re-add, cf. [[r3-domain-core-kernel-walls]] discipline). **Residual blueprint cleanup left for a focused pass:** `thm:codim_one_extension`'s proof prose + `\uses{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay}` still describe the old Auslander–Buchsbaum/Cohen–Macaulay route, but the honest `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete` uses the valuative criterion and needs no 0AVF (memory: 0AVF/`cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` phantom in the Milne chain). Rewriting that proof prose is the remaining consistency debt.

echo "=== actual code sorries (:= sorry / by sorry / => sorry / indented bare sorry), excluding comments ==="
grep -nE '(:=[[:space:]]*sorry|[[:space:]]by[[:space:]]+sorry|=>[[:space:]]*sorry|^[[:space:]]+sorry[[:space:]]*$)' AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean | grep -vE '^\s*[0-9]+:\s*(--|/-|\*)' | head
echo "(empty = no code sorry)"
echo ""
echo "=== force-rebuild AuslanderBuchsbaum to see any sorry warning fresh ==="
touch AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean
timeout 200 lake build AlgebraicJacobian.Albanese.AuslanderBuchsbaum 2>&1 | grep -icE 'declaration uses .sorry.'
echo "^ count of sorry-warnings (0 = sorry-free)"
input.description: Precisely confirm AuslanderBuchsbaum sorry-free via forced rebuild

text:
All work is complete and clean: no stray files, the new module builds (8558 jobs), and both commits landed. Here is my report.

## Summary

The T9 rational-map-extension **port had already landed on disk** (all 8 `Albanese/*.lean` files byte-identical to the subproject, wired into the root) and was captured in the run-0013 baseline `3029a02d` — so it was already recorded, not mine to commit. I verified it end-to-end, then determined via an authoritative parallel investigation that **both remaining Albanese legs are gated on large greenfield sub-builds, not one-session work**. I added a reusable axiom-clean bridge substrate and repaired two real blueprint↔Lean drifts.

## Progress
- AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/RationalMapFunctionField.lean: NEW — added `Scheme.RationalMap.stalkPullback` (axiom-clean germ pullback `𝒪_{Y,f(η_X)}→K(X)`); dominant `K(Y)→K(X)` refinement documented, not closed.
- AlgebraicJacobian.lean: added the root import wiring for the new module (only change vs baseline).
- blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex: replaced 3 stale nodes pinning deleted `CodimOneFree`-route decls with one honest `av_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty` node; fixed the main `\uses`.
- blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex: re-pinned `thm:codim_one_extension` off the deleted, false `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` → `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete`; deleted its false "CodimOneFree ⇒ extension" paragraph.
- AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean: unchanged — its 1 real sorry (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`, :1662) needs Milne 3.3 substeps 1/2/4b (greenfield), not closable this session.
- AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/AlbaneseUP.lean: unchanged — 7 substrate sorries all downstream of the missing `Sym^g` scheme substrate + A.3; `albanese_universal_property` is already a sorry-free assembly.

## Verification
- `lake build` green (8573 jobs) over the whole ported extension leg + UP file. New module builds (8558 jobs), kernel-verified **axiom-clean** (`[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`).
- Kernel `#print axioms`: support files (SmoothPrimeRegularity, StandardSmoothDimension) axiom-clean; `extend_to_av` and `albanese_universal_property` carry `sorryAx` (via CodimOne:1662 / substrate sorries). Corrected an earlier miscount: **AuslanderBuchsbaum is sorry-free** (forced rebuild → 0 sorry warnings; the "sorry" greps were stale docstring prose).
- Blueprint regenerated: 0 dangling; both edited nodes resolve; no `\lean{}`/`\cref` left pinning any removed declaration.
- Commits: `e9e95095` (bridge + blueprint fixes), `48f2e3e0` (root import). Memory `t9-albanese-endgame-unblock-map` + MEMORY.md index + comment on inbox `I-0112`.

## Issues
- **No sorry was closed.** The port was pre-landed; the germ-pullback bridge is pure infrastructure (the investigation confirmed it discharges no existing sorry — Milne 3.3 still needs substeps 1/2/4b, and `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction` is unpinned).
- **Did not run a full-project `lake build AlgebraicJacobian`** (heavy). My Lean change is a new leaf module (built + axiom-clean) + a trivially-correct import line; it cannot break the existing build.
- **Dominant field-hom (`functionFieldPullback`) reverted** — walls on `IrreducibleSpace g.domain.toScheme` (doesn't auto-synthesise) and the `↥g.domain` vs `↥↑g.domain` Opens-carrier coercion; exact recipe left in the file's docstring.
- **Residual blueprint debt (deferred):** `thm:codim_one_extension`'s proof prose + `\uses{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay}` still describe the old Cohen–Macaulay route (phantom in the Milne chain per memory I-0065); fixing needs a proof-prose rewrite, recorded in memory.
- The workflow's substep-4b investigation agent errored (structured-output retry cap) — informational only; I already understand 4b is blocked.

## Why I stopped
Task not complete: the Albanese universal property is far from formalized. Both legs are genuinely blocked on multi-session greenfield sub-builds — the extension leg on Milne 3.3 substeps 1/2/4b, and the UP-assembly leg on the **missing `Sym^g` scheme substrate** (Mathlib v4.31 has no scheme-level symmetric power / geometric quotient / Noether finiteness of invariants). Neither is closable in one session, so no further sorry is reachable in scope. I maximized in-session value instead: verified and recorded the landed port, added the axiom-clean function-field germ-pullback substrate the project lacked, repaired two real blueprint drifts (including removing a forbidden false statement), and left a precise unblock map.

## Next
- Extension leg: build Milne 3.3 substeps 1 (difference map `Φ:X×X⇢G`), 2 (slice), 4b (diagonal codim-1 Krull bound); finish the dominant `functionFieldPullback` (recipe in `RationalMapFunctionField.lean`) to feed substep 3.
- UP leg: build the `Sym^g C` scheme substrate (Milne III.3, glue `Spec((A^{⊗g})^{S_g})`) — the single largest unblock for `AlbaneseUP.lean`.
- Blueprint: rewrite `thm:codim_one_extension`'s proof prose to the valuative-criterion argument and drop the phantom `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` `\uses`.
